mcc_001.title: Flash strike
mcc_001.body: Employees at the logistics centers announce a surprise strike. Deliveries are delayed.

choices.A: Open negotiations and promise a bonus.
choices.B: Hold the line: temp workers and forced overtime.
choices.C: Suspend secondary shipments and communicate clearly.

mcc_002.title: Surprise audit
mcc_002.body: A regulatory authority shows up for a full compliance audit. Your teams are panicking.

choices.A: Fully cooperate, open the files, and fix issues quickly.
choices.B: Play for time with lawyers and deadlines.
choices.C: Announce a public compliance program before the audit.

mcc_003.title: Data leak
mcc_003.body: A server was misconfigured: customer data was exposed.

choices.A: Inform people immediately
choices.B: Wait until you confirm the exact impact before speaking.
choices.C: Downplay it publicly and handle it internally.

mcc_004.title: Packaging too expensive
mcc_004.body: The new premium packaging is popular, but it is very expensive and slows down production.

choices.A: Go back to standard packaging, style be damned.
choices.B: Raise prices slightly to absorb the cost.
choices.C: Keep the packaging and automate the line.

mcc_005.title: Unpredictable influencer
mcc_005.body: A famous influencer offers a partnership. They are talented... and completely uncontrollable.

choices.A: Sign anyway: maximum visibility.
choices.C: Sign, but with guidelines and strict content control.

mcc_006.title: Remote work on the verge of a clash
mcc_006.body: Managers want people back in the office. Teams are threatening to leave if you force too much.

choices.A: Flexible hybrid model, team by team.
choices.C: Almost fully remote work + shared offices.

mcc_007.title: Drop in quality
mcc_007.body: Customer complaints are exploding around a flagship product. The quality team wants the line stopped.

choices.A: Stop production and fix it before restarting.
choices.B: Keep going and deal with it as you go.
choices.C: Launch a free replacement program and tighten controls without stopping.

mcc_008.title: Factory flooded
mcc_008.body: Torrential rain floods a production site. The machines are out of service.

choices.A: Accept the slowdown, secure the site, and help the teams.
choices.B: Temporarily move production to an expensive subcontractor.
choices.C: Announce a strong resilience plan and ask clients for more time.

mcc_009.title: Rumor of a takeover attempt
mcc_009.body: A rumor of a hostile takeover drives the stock up. Investors love it. Employees panic.

choices.A: Defend yourself: stock buyback and anti-takeover plan.
choices.B: Negotiate a "friendly" merger behind the scenes.
choices.C: Ignore it publicly and keep going as if nothing happened.

mcc_010.title: Bonus or research
mcc_010.body: The board demands a choice: shareholder bonuses or major research investment. You have to decide.

choices.A: Immediate bonus for shareholders.
choices.B: Invest heavily in research, profits later.
choices.C: Compromise: small bonus + targeted research.

mcc_011.title: Poisoned gift
mcc_011.body: A major client offers a huge contract... with a massive penalty if you miss a deadline.

choices.A: Sign: ambitious target, everyone on deck.
choices.C: Sign, but negotiate the clause and spread out the schedule.

mcc_012.title: Energy cost spike
mcc_012.body: The energy bill is exploding across multiple sites. Margins are melting away.

choices.A: Invest in energy efficiency for the long term.
choices.B: Pass part of the cost on to customers through prices.
choices.C: Cut costs: minimal heating, reduced lighting

mcc_013.title: Accused of greenwashing
mcc_013.body: Your latest "sustainability" report is under attack: "nice words, little proof."

choices.A: Publish the raw numbers and accept the criticism.
choices.B: Launch an ad campaign to bury the issue.
choices.C: Request an independent audit and update the report.

mcc_014.title: Rushed launch
mcc_014.body: Your product team wants to launch a test version tomorrow.

choices.A: Launch tomorrow and fix things as you go.
choices.C: Launch limited access to a few pilot customers.

mcc_015.title: Platform outage
mcc_015.body: A global outage blocks your service for 3 hours. Social media is on fire.

choices.A: Explain, apologize, and offer compensation.
choices.B: Say it was "an isolated incident" and move on.
choices.C: Blame a supplier and publicly demand answers.

mcc_016.title: A key talent leaves
mcc_016.body: Your head of tech announces they are leaving. Teams panic. Investors are watching.

choices.A: Offer a huge counterproposal.
choices.B: Respect the decision and promote internal talent.
choices.C: Bring in an expensive outside star.

mcc_017.title: Internal leak
mcc_017.body: An internal document ends up online. It shows "aggressive" cost-cutting goals.

choices.A: Own it and explain the plan clearly.
choices.C: Publicly punish the person behind the leak.

mcc_018.title: Training program
mcc_018.body: HR is asking for a big budget to train everyone. The CFO is grimacing.

choices.A: Approve it: broad upskilling.
choices.B: Decline: targeted training at the bare minimum.
choices.C: Fund half of it, with the rest through supervised self-learning.

mcc_019.title: Failed ad campaign
mcc_019.body: A "humorous" ad is being mocked as outdated. It becomes a meme... not necessarily a flattering one.

choices.A: Pull the ad and apologize.
choices.B: Own the meme and ride the wave.
choices.C: Shut it down: total silence, no comment.

mcc_020.title: Safety inspection
mcc_020.body: An inspection reveals outdated safety procedures at one site. You need to act.

choices.A: Upgrade immediately, with a partial site shutdown.
choices.B: Make it a 6-month plan without disrupting production.
choices.C: Outsource safety management to an outside company.

mcc_021.title: Price war
mcc_021.body: A competitor slashes prices aggressively. Your sales team wants an immediate response.

choices.A: Lower prices to defend market share.
choices.B: Hold prices and strengthen value, service, and quality.
choices.C: Launch a visible limited-time offer.

mcc_022.title: Questionable subcontractor
mcc_022.body: An internal report says your main subcontractor is "cheating" on standards.

choices.A: Break the contract, even if it is expensive.
choices.B: Audit quietly before making a move.
choices.C: Ignore it: if it works, it works.

mcc_023.title: Internal reorganization
mcc_023.body: Your company structure has become a maze. Decisions take weeks.

choices.A: Simplify: fewer managers, more autonomous teams.
choices.B: Keep the structure: stability above all.
choices.C: Create a temporary task force to unblock decisions.

mcc_024.title: Customer complaint
mcc_024.body: Customers accuse your support team of being "robotic and cold." You are being asked for a response.

choices.A: Hire and train more human support staff.
choices.B: Improve support AI and reduce wait times.
choices.C: Offer compensation to the people who complained.

mcc_025.title: New market
mcc_025.body: An opportunity to expand into a new country opens up.

choices.A: Move fast with a small local team.
choices.B: Wait and prepare a perfect launch.
choices.C: Partner with an established local player.

mcc_026.title: Wellness program
mcc_026.body: HR proposes a wellness program, mental health support, and rest time.

choices.A: Approve it and communicate about it.
choices.C: Test it on just one pilot department.

mcc_027.title: Anonymous complaint
mcc_027.body: An anonymous complaint mentions "abusive pressure" in one team. Nothing concrete, but the rumor is growing.

choices.A: Open an internal investigation with a neutral third party.
choices.B: Talk to the manager and tell them to calm down, period.
choices.C: Ignore it: no proof, no problem.

mcc_028.title: Automation
mcc_028.body: Automating one site would bring big gains, but jobs would disappear. The unions are watching you.

choices.A: Automate fully and offer retraining.
choices.C: Automate partially, step by step.

mcc_029.title: Supply shortage
mcc_029.body: A key component becomes impossible to find for 2 months. Your teams are improvising shaky solutions.

choices.A: Pay more for a reliable alternative supplier.
choices.C: Change the product design to work around the component.

mcc_030.title: PR stunt
mcc_030.body: Your communications team wants a flashy move: a spectacular promise and a catchy slogan.

choices.A: Approve it: make an impression.
choices.C: Approve it, but with careful wording.

mcc_031.title: Gifts for clients
mcc_031.body: Your sales team wants to offer "high-end" gifts to major clients.

choices.A: Allow it: that is how the game is played.
choices.C: Allow it, but with a registry, cap, and transparency.

mcc_032.title: Secret project
mcc_032.body: A "secret" project could be revolutionary... or a money pit.

choices.A: Fund it fully, no half measures.
choices.C: Fund it in stages, with strict checkpoints.

choices.A: Pay to secure the chain, now.
choices.B: Refuse and look for an alternative.
choices.C: Buy the supplier to save it, and control it.

mcc_034.title: Top talent poached
mcc_034.body: A competitor is trying to poach your best-performing team, all at once.

choices.A: Raise salaries and offer stock.
choices.B: Let them go: we will hire new talent.
choices.C: Offer more autonomy and a motivating project, without huge bonuses.

mcc_035.title: Regulator request
mcc_035.body: A regulatory authority asks for detailed data on your business practices within 10 days.

choices.A: Send everything, even if it reveals weaknesses.
choices.B: Negotiate more time and stick to the strict legal minimum.
choices.C: Launch an internal overhaul and officially ask for time.

mcc_036.title: Call for moderation
mcc_036.body: Part of the public criticizes "overconsumption." Your company is named as a symbol of it.

choices.A: Launch a repair and secondhand program.
choices.B: Respond with a campaign: "we create jobs."
choices.C: Do nothing: it will pass.

mcc_037.title: Layoff plan
mcc_037.body: The CFO proposes layoffs to reassure the market. The teams are on edge.

choices.A: Accept: profitability first.
choices.B: Decline: bet on growth and team loyalty.
choices.C: Freeze hiring and cut spending without layoffs.

mcc_038.title: Patent lawsuit
mcc_038.body: A company specialized in patent litigation is suing you over an old technical detail.

choices.A: Pay to settle quickly.
choices.B: Fight it in court to the end.
choices.C: Negotiate a deal + launch a legal counterattack.

mcc_039.title: Billing bug
mcc_039.body: A bug charged some customers twice. Most have not noticed... but a few are shouting.

choices.A: Automatically refund everyone, with apologies.
choices.C: Offer future credit instead of a refund.

mcc_040.title: Gift for employees
mcc_040.body: It is the end of the year. Teams are expecting a gesture. The board would prefer an "inspiring" message.

choices.A: Special bonus for everyone.
choices.C: Targeted bonus for the teams under the most pressure.

mcc_041.title: Return policy
mcc_041.body: Customers want a simpler return policy. Finance fears abuse.

choices.A: Simplify it a lot: free and easy returns.
choices.B: Tighten it: checks and return fees.
choices.C: Simplify it, but with smart limits, thresholds, and history.

mcc_042.title: Pay transparency
mcc_042.body: An internal movement is demanding more pay transparency.

choices.A: Publish salary ranges by role and a leveling plan.
choices.C: Test transparency in just one division.

mcc_043.title: Polluting express shipping
mcc_043.body: The "ultra-fast delivery" option is booming, but it greatly increases trips and emissions.

choices.A: Limit ultra-fast delivery to essential products.
choices.B: Keep it as is: customers love it.
choices.C: Keep it, but add a paid "offset" option.

mcc_044.title: Board meeting
mcc_044.body: The board wants a 12-month plan. They want it "simple, profitable, and sexy."

choices.A: Profitable plan: cuts and focus on the core business.
choices.C: Balanced plan: profitability + targeted innovation.

mcc_045.title: Camera at work
mcc_045.body: An internal video shows employees being filmed without meaning to during an event. General discomfort.

choices.A: Ban unauthorized videos and strengthen privacy.
choices.B: Say it was a mistake and move on.
choices.C: Create clear rules + training + image rights policy.

mcc_046.title: New ethics charter
mcc_046.body: Your legal team proposes a strict ethics charter. Some "borderline" sales would disappear.

choices.A: Adopt the charter as is.
choices.C: Adopt it, but with controlled, documented exceptions.

mcc_047.title: Idea contest
mcc_047.body: Employees want an idea contest with a budget. Investors ask: "What does it bring in?"

choices.A: Launch the contest with a real budget.
choices.C: Launch a contest with no budget, just recognition.

mcc_048.title: Logo change
mcc_048.body: The creative team wants to modernize the logo.

choices.A: Change it completely: a fresh start.
choices.C: Light evolution: gentle modernization.

mcc_049.title: Notice of fine
mcc_049.body: You receive notice of a fine for a practice considered questionable. You can challenge it or fix it.

choices.A: Pay and fix it immediately.
choices.C: Fix it right away and negotiate a reduction.

mcc_050.title: Extravagant seminar
mcc_050.body: Your team proposes an "unforgettable" seminar at a luxury venue.

choices.A: Approve it: team cohesion matters.
choices.B: Cancel it: budget and image first.
choices.C: Do a modest seminar, but offer extra free time.

mcc_051.title: Leaked meeting
mcc_051.body: An internal audio clip is circulating: people hear you say "cut costs, even if it hurts."

choices.A: Own it and explain the context publicly.
choices.B: Deny it and threaten legal action over the leak.
choices.C: Make an internal apology video + adjust the plan.

mcc_052.title: Sleeping inventory
mcc_052.body: A warehouse is full of unsold stock. Investors want a plan before the next report.

choices.A: Huge immediate sale, even if margins take a hit.
choices.B: Destroy/remove it quietly and move on.
choices.C: Turn it into donations + image campaign.

mcc_053.title: Customer service overwhelmed
mcc_053.body: Support cannot keep up anymore. Response times double, online reviews are dropping.

choices.A: Hire and quickly train reinforcements.
choices.B: Automate more heavily with AI to absorb demand.
choices.C: Prioritize critical cases and compensate the rest.

mcc_054.title: Email ban
mcc_054.body: An internal audit shows email has become a graveyard of decisions. Everyone is complaining.

choices.A: Impose strict rules: fewer emails.
choices.B: Let it be: that is company culture.
choices.C: Launch an internal decision tool + training.

mcc_055.title: New local tax
mcc_055.body: A region announces a new tax on your activity. The local site could become unprofitable.

choices.A: Negotiate with authorities, play the cooperation card.
choices.B: Threaten to leave if the tax stays.
choices.C: Pass part of the cost on through prices in that region.

mcc_056.title: Product too addictive
mcc_056.body: Media outlets criticize your product: "too designed to hook people." It is becoming a debate.

choices.A: Add control and limit options, then communicate about it.
choices.C: Change the algorithm quietly, without announcing it.

mcc_057.title: Payroll delay
mcc_057.body: A payroll bug threatens to delay part of your employees' salaries. Panic.

choices.A: Pay early via an exceptional transfer.
choices.C: Offer salary advances on request.

mcc_058.title: Research or quick gain
mcc_058.body: Your new lab is expensive and will not pay off for 18 months. The market wants results.

choices.A: Scale back the lab, secure the short term.
choices.B: Double the investment and own the gamble.
choices.C: Look for outside co-funding.

mcc_059.title: Exemplary employer label
mcc_059.body: You are offered an employer label in exchange for a large communication budget. It may end up looking like a bought award.

choices.A: Decline: improve reality first.
choices.C: Negotiate a real audit before any label is granted

mcc_060.title: Delivery accident
mcc_060.body: A route was badly planned: several delivery drivers end up with impossible workdays.

choices.A: Revise targets and strengthen planning.
choices.B: Stay the course: "we keep the customer promise."
choices.C: Offer a one-time bonus to the teams involved.

mcc_061.title: Quiet product recall
mcc_061.body: A rare defect exists in one batch. You can do a quiet recall or a public one.

choices.A: Public recall, full transparency.
choices.C: Wait: low probability, keep monitoring.

mcc_062.title: Ad team out of control
mcc_062.body: The ad team wants an aggressive slogan: "We crush the competition." Legal sighs.

choices.A: Approve it: psychological warfare.
choices.C: Keep the energy but change the wording.

mcc_063.title: University partnership
mcc_063.body: A university offers a research partnership. It is slow, but prestigious.

choices.A: Sign: long term + image.
choices.C: Sign for a short pilot project.

mcc_064.title: Contested bonus system
mcc_064.body: The current bonus system is seen as unfair: too opaque, too variable, too "political."

choices.A: Make the system transparent with clear criteria.
choices.B: Change nothing: that is just how it is.
choices.C: Add a collective share + keep an individual share.

mcc_065.title: Criticism over diversity
mcc_065.body: The company is being accused of being too homogeneous in leadership. It is starting to show.

choices.A: Concrete plan: hiring + promotion + tracked metrics.
choices.C: Ignore it: business comes first.

mcc_066.title: Internet outage
mcc_066.body: A regional internet outage blocks one site. Teams can no longer work.

choices.A: Invest in a backup connection immediately.
choices.C: Give paid time off to the affected teams.

mcc_067.title: Internal award
mcc_067.body: An employee invents a tool that saves time. Teams want to reward them publicly.

choices.A: Bonus + promotion, and scale the tool.
choices.C: Symbolic bonus + dedicated team to industrialize it.

mcc_068.title: Public contract
mcc_068.body: A huge public contract is possible, but the administrative file is heavy and tightly controlled.

choices.A: Go for it: dedicated compliance team.
choices.C: Go for it through a partner that is already approved.

mcc_069.title: Competitor copied your product
mcc_069.body: A competitor launches a product similar to yours. The teams want a response.

choices.A: Sue them.
choices.C: Cut prices and crush the market.

mcc_070.title: CEO phone
mcc_070.body: Your phone leaks notifications during a public event. Speculation starts flying.

choices.A: Laugh it off and deflect: "yes, I work."
choices.B: Refuse to comment, shut it down.
choices.C: Publish a clarification and calm things down.

mcc_071.title: New standard
mcc_071.body: A new technical standard is coming in 6 months. You can anticipate or wait.

choices.A: Anticipate now, full migration.
choices.C: Anticipate only on critical products.

mcc_072.title: Team under pressure
mcc_072.body: A team is exhausted. There is no open crisis yet, but the fatigue is real and results are falling.

choices.A: Lower targets and increase headcount.
choices.B: Demand that they keep going despite the fatigue.
choices.C: Put in a crisis manager + rotations.

mcc_073.title: Surprise visit
mcc_073.body: Investors ask for a surprise factory visit. The site is... not at its best this month.

choices.A: Accept and be transparent.
choices.C: Emergency cleanup + staging.

mcc_074.title: Refund policy
mcc_074.body: Finance wants to tighten refunds. Support says your reviews will get destroyed.

choices.A: Loosen it: customer satisfaction first.
choices.C: Segment it: flexible for loyal customers, strict for others.

mcc_075.title: IT migration
mcc_075.body: Your infrastructure needs to migrate to a new platform. Risk of bugs, but major long-term gains.

choices.A: Gradual, secure migration.
choices.C: Push it back: too risky this year.

mcc_076.title: Debate over wages
mcc_076.body: Starting salaries are seen as too low. Recruiting is difficult, and internal tensions are rising.

choices.A: Raise starting salaries.
choices.B: Keep them as is, make up for it with perks.
choices.C: Raise them + fast progression plan.

mcc_077.title: Quarterly report
mcc_077.body: You can announce clean but cautious numbers, or aggressive targets that make people dream.

choices.A: Cautious and reliable.
choices.C: Ambitious, but with guardrails.

mcc_078.title: Office too luxurious
mcc_078.body: An article criticizes your headquarters: "marble and armchairs while the field struggles."

choices.A: Open headquarters to the teams and cut visible spending.
choices.B: Ignore it: it is your image of power.
choices.C: Invest more in frontline operations and show it.

choices.A: Push them with penalties.
choices.C: Urgently diversify with a second subcontractor.

mcc_080.title: International conference
mcc_080.body: You are invited to speak. Great exposure, but every sentence will be scrutinized.

choices.A: Transparent speech with numbers.
choices.C: Short speech + controlled Q&A.

mcc_081.title: Obsolete internal software
mcc_081.body: An old internal software system is slowing everyone down. Replacing it is expensive, but keeping it wastes time.

choices.A: Replace it now.
choices.C: Replace it step by step, department by department.

mcc_082.title: Executive bonus
mcc_082.body: Executives ask for a special bonus. Frontline employees view it badly.

choices.A: Bonus for everyone, smaller amount.
choices.C: Executive bonus + training budget for everyone.

mcc_083.title: Too many meetings
mcc_083.body: Teams say they spend their whole lives in meetings.

choices.A: Ban certain meetings, strict rules.
choices.C: Create weekly "no meeting" days.

mcc_084.title: New quality label
mcc_084.body: A recognized quality label is available, but it requires time and regular audits.

choices.A: Go for it: excellence.
choices.B: Do not pursue it: too expensive.
choices.C: Start with a partial certification.

mcc_085.title: Aggressive sales team
mcc_085.body: Sales are up, but customers complain about overly pushy practices.

choices.A: Realign sales: charter + oversight.
choices.C: Bonus based on customer satisfaction, not just volume.

mcc_086.title: Noisy construction site
mcc_086.body: Construction near one site is making logistics difficult. Delays are piling up.

choices.A: Negotiate with the city to adjust schedules.
choices.B: Temporarily move part of the operations.
choices.C: Push the teams to make up for it with overtime.

mcc_087.title: App too heavy
mcc_087.body: Your app is getting slow. Users are complaining, but product wants to add more features.

choices.A: Pause: performance optimization first.
choices.C: Optimize part of it + limited new features.

mcc_088.title: Open-source debate
mcc_088.body: Engineers want to open part of the code. Legal and investors hesitate.

choices.A: Open it: attract talent and tech credibility.
choices.C: Open only non-strategic tools.

mcc_089.title: Complaint from a major client
mcc_089.body: A major client threatens to leave unless you change one contract clause. It would hurt revenue.

choices.A: Give in: keep the client.
choices.C: Compromise: adjust it in exchange for a longer commitment.

mcc_090.title: Line shutdown
mcc_090.body: A production line has too many small breakdowns. You can stop it for major maintenance.

choices.A: Stop it and repair it properly.
choices.B: Keep going and manage it day by day.
choices.C: Night maintenance + reduced production.

mcc_091.title: Internal survey
mcc_091.body: An internal survey shows declining trust in leadership. That stings.

choices.A: Respond with a clear plan and dates.
choices.C: Hold listening meetings, then act.

mcc_092.title: Cost hunt
mcc_092.body: The CFO wants to cut "non-essential" budgets: events, tools, and small expenses.

choices.A: Cut hard and fast.
choices.C: Targeted cuts + reinvest in frontline operations.

mcc_093.title: Physical security
mcc_093.body: Weak access controls are reported at one site. Nothing serious, but it looks bad.

choices.A: Strengthen immediately, strict controls.
choices.B: Leave it: incident risk is low.
choices.C: Strengthen it, but without becoming too rigid.

mcc_094.title: Rumor of a price increase
mcc_094.body: A rumor says you are going to raise prices. Customers are getting upset before the announcement.

choices.A: Clarify quickly and transparently.
choices.C: Delay the increase and calm the market.

mcc_095.title: Legal team overloaded
mcc_095.body: Legal is overwhelmed. Contracts take weeks, and sales is complaining.

choices.A: Hire and modernize the processes.
choices.B: Apply pressure: "you need to move faster."
choices.C: Standardize contract templates + automate.

mcc_096.title: Huge order
mcc_096.body: A massive order comes in. You can accept it, but it will twist your whole production for a month.

choices.A: Accept it: immediate growth.
choices.C: Accept part of it, spread it out over time.

mcc_097.title: Managers' reputation
mcc_097.body: Some managers are seen as too "harsh." Results are good, atmosphere is average.

choices.A: Train the managers and set boundaries.
choices.B: Keep things as they are: performance first.
choices.C: Replace a few key managers, not all.

mcc_098.title: Gimmick innovation
mcc_098.body: A fun feature is being requested. It is not very useful... but it gets people talking.

choices.A: Launch it: media buzz.
choices.C: Launch it as an option, without disturbing the core.

mcc_099.title: Expense control
mcc_099.body: Expense reports are getting out of hand. Nothing scandalous, but costs are rising.

choices.A: Strict rules and mandatory approval.
choices.B: Leave it alone: controls cost more.
choices.C: Simple caps + random checks.

mcc_100.title: Transparency charter
mcc_100.body: You can adopt a transparency charter for your practices.

choices.A: Fully adopt it and publish regular reports.
choices.C: Adopt a light, gradual version.

mcc_101.title: Revolutionary coffee machine
mcc_101.body: The new coffee machine only serves decaf. The whole headquarters is complaining.

choices.A: Refund it + install a simple, reliable machine.
choices.B: Say it is an official "caffeine detox."
choices.C: Open an internal barista station... sponsored by communications.

mcc_102.title: Infinite PowerPoint
mcc_102.body: A 212-slide presentation is scheduled. Half the employees are looking for an emergency exit.

choices.A: Limit it to 15 slides max and require a summary.
choices.C: Replace it with a short video + Q&A.

mcc_103.title: Happiness indicator
mcc_103.body: A manager proposes a "smiles per minute" indicator. Teams are not sure if it is a joke.

choices.A: Decline and launch a real wellness survey.
choices.B: Accept it for image purposes, without measuring it.
choices.C: Accept it, but just for fun + no penalties.

mcc_104.title: Embarrassing mascot
mcc_104.body: The new brand mascot looks way too much like a competitor's. Way too much.

choices.A: Change the mascot immediately and own it.
choices.B: Deny it: "it is a generic animal."
choices.C: Keep it, but redesign it heavily.

mcc_105.title: Standing meeting
mcc_105.body: Headquarters is testing standing meetings "to move faster." Result: more meetings, less energy.

choices.A: Stop the experiment and reduce meetings.
choices.C: Keep it only for emergencies, 10 minutes max.

mcc_106.title: Fridge hack
mcc_106.body: The connected fridge got "hacked": it orders 400 yogurts. Storage is overflowing.

choices.A: Cut the gadgets, secure things, and give away the yogurts.
choices.B: Turn it into a campaign: "we feed the future."
choices.C: Keep the fridge, but with strict purchase controls.

mcc_107.title: Email to everyone
mcc_107.body: An employee sends a private message... to the whole company. It is embarrassing. And funny. For everyone else.

choices.A: Train people on tools + simple communication rules.
choices.C: Laugh it off, but add technical safeguards.

mcc_108.title: Surprise dress code
mcc_108.body: One executive wants to impose more formal clothing.

choices.A: Cancel the dress code: comfort and trust.
choices.C: Leave it open except for official events.

mcc_109.title: Ambiguous slogan
mcc_109.body: The new international slogan translates badly in one language. Very badly.

choices.A: Pull it fast and fix it with apologies.
choices.B: Keep going: "it will get people talking."
choices.C: Adapt it by country + linguistic validation.

mcc_110.title: Extreme team activity
mcc_110.body: You are offered a team-building "forest survival" activity.

choices.A: Do something simple and inclusive.
choices.B: Approve it: "step out of the comfort zone."
choices.C: Approve it, but as an option with a lighter version.

mcc_111.title: Printer war
mcc_111.body: The printer refuses everything. "Error 0x..." Nobody knows what it means. Neither does HQ.

choices.A: Replace it + standardize the equipment.
choices.C: Train point people on each floor + dedicated IT support.

mcc_112.title: Innovation label
mcc_112.body: A consulting firm offers an “Innovative Company” label in exchange for a very high bill. It may end up looking like a paid distinction.

choices.A: Decline and invest in a real innovation project.
choices.C: Accept only if a serious audit is included.

mcc_113.title: Too many English words
mcc_113.body: An internal memo is 70% English. Teams are asking for a translator... or a dictionary.

choices.A: Require clear, simple communication.
choices.C: Allow it, but with a plain-language summary.

mcc_114.title: Ergonomic chair
mcc_114.body: Employees want ergonomic chairs. Finance proposes "stretching."

choices.A: Buy decent chairs for everyone.
choices.C: Pilot it on the most exposed workstations.

mcc_115.title: Ping-pong at the office
mcc_115.body: A ping-pong table is installed. Result: productivity drops... but morale rises.

choices.A: Keep it: supervised breaks, balance.
choices.C: Keep it, but only during certain time slots.

mcc_116.title: Visionary boss
mcc_116.body: An executive wants to rename every department with bold names.

choices.A: Stop it: stay serious.
choices.B: Approve it: maybe it motivates people.
choices.C: Allow it internally only, in a fun way.

mcc_117.title: Events budget
mcc_117.body: The events budget is exploding. You are being asked whether a giant chandelier is "strategic."

choices.A: Cut it and reallocate to frontline operations.
choices.C: Keep it, but cap it strictly.

mcc_118.title: Collector product
mcc_118.body: The ad team wants a useless collector's edition that would be perfect for social media.

choices.A: Decline: utility first.
choices.B: Approve it: media buzz and quick sales.
choices.C: Approve it in small quantities, without disrupting production.

mcc_119.title: Mic on during a video call
mcc_119.body: During a company-wide video call, your mic stays on.

choices.A: Own it, laugh, and simplify.
choices.B: Ignore it and move to the next slide.
choices.C: Apologize internally and announce a clarity plan.

mcc_120.title: Goals board
mcc_120.body: A goal board is posted everywhere. Teams say it looks like a wall of threats.

choices.A: Revise the goals with realistic buffers.
choices.C: Keep it, but add recognition and support.

mcc_121.title: Prodigy intern
mcc_121.body: An intern automates a task that 12 people used to do by hand. Applause... and anxiety.

choices.A: Reward them and reassign the work to better tasks.
choices.C: Roll it out gradually with an upskilling plan.

mcc_122.title: Email from the regulator
mcc_122.body: A regulator sends a polite email... ending with "please reply within 7 days."

choices.A: Reply quickly, fully, and fix what needs fixing.
choices.C: Ask for more time + launch an internal audit.

mcc_123.title: VIP complaint
mcc_123.body: A VIP client is complaining about a tiny detail. But they have a lot of friends and a lot of influence.

choices.A: Fix it quickly + offer compensation.
choices.C: Fix it without compensation, but with careful communication.

mcc_124.title: Internal challenge
mcc_124.body: An internal contest begins to reward the best sales. It motivates people, but also creates tension.

choices.A: Keep it, but add ethical and quality criteria.
choices.C: Keep it team-based, not individual.

choices.A: Create rules: urgent vs non-urgent.
choices.B: Let it go: noise is life.
choices.C: Make silent mode mandatory during certain time slots.

mcc_126.title: Name change
mcc_126.body: You are being offered a company rename to seem more "modern."

choices.A: Decline: stability.
choices.C: Make it a light, gradual evolution.

mcc_127.title: Open space vs pods
mcc_127.body: Employees want quiet spaces. Investors want to "increase density."

choices.A: Create quiet pods + noise rules.
choices.C: Hybrid: quiet zones + collaboration zones.

mcc_128.title: x10 price bug
mcc_128.body: For 20 minutes, your site shows prices multiplied by 10. Screenshots are circulating.

choices.A: Apologize + explain + offer something to calm people down.
choices.B: Say it was a test and shut the discussion down.
choices.C: Joke about it: "luxury version," then fix and clarify.

mcc_129.title: Moving boxes
mcc_129.body: Headquarters moves. The boxes stay in the hallway for 3 months. Everyone is living in Tetris.

choices.A: Storage plan and team accountability.
choices.C: Bring in a pro team + sorting.

mcc_130.title: Committee of committees
mcc_130.body: To decide on one issue, a committee is created. Then a committee for that committee. Employees have stopped believing.

choices.A: Remove layers, make decisions more direct.
choices.C: Keep it, but limit it to 2 levels max.

mcc_131.title: Logo on everything
mcc_131.body: The ad team is slapping the logo everywhere: mugs, chairs, elevators.

choices.A: Stop putting the logo everywhere
choices.C: Keep the logo only on a few premium items.

mcc_132.title: Red button
mcc_132.body: A red "EMERGENCY" button has been added to an internal tool. Everyone clicks it for trivial things.

choices.A: Create urgency levels + light penalties.
choices.C: Keep it, but limit it to certain roles.

mcc_133.title: Express hiring
mcc_133.body: One department wants to hire 30 people in 2 weeks.

choices.A: Hire gradually, quality first.
choices.C: Speed up, but with stronger training.

mcc_134.title: The diplomatic elevator
mcc_134.body: You get stuck in an elevator with an authority figure, an investor, and an employee representative.

choices.A: Promise transparency and balance to everyone.
choices.C: Reassure employees + compliance first.

mcc_135.title: Report too honest
mcc_135.body: Your team wrote an honest internal report. Too honest. It says: "we improvise a lot."

choices.A: Own it and launch an improvement plan.
choices.C: Keep the honesty, but control distribution.

mcc_136.title: Luxury restrooms
mcc_136.body: A rumor says the executive floor has luxury restrooms.

choices.A: Make everything equivalent, period.
choices.C: Gradually renovate all floors.

mcc_137.title: No-email day
mcc_137.body: A no-email day is proposed. Some people think it is impossible.

choices.A: Test it: one day a week, clear rules.
choices.C: Do a half-day without email.

mcc_138.title: Product too perfect
mcc_138.body: The product is so optimized it has no little flaws anymore... and customers find it "cold."

choices.A: Humanize it: tone, support, small touches.
choices.C: Add an optional "customized" mode.

mcc_139.title: Internal newsletter
mcc_139.body: The internal newsletter is 18 pages long. Almost nobody really reads it.

choices.A: Switch to a short, useful format.
choices.C: Keep it, but add a very short summary at the beginning.

mcc_140.title: Guest guru
mcc_140.body: You are offered a management "guru." They charge a lot and speak in riddles.

choices.A: Decline: invest in practical training.
choices.C: Accept one test session, then decide.

mcc_141.title: Cost-cutting hunt
mcc_141.body: Finance proposes eliminating pens. Result: everyone steals partner pens.

choices.A: Restore basic supplies, and cut elsewhere.
choices.C: Limit it with a simple budget per team.

mcc_142.title: Wall of values
mcc_142.body: Headquarters wants a giant values wall. Employees ask: "can we get the budget in salaries instead?"

choices.A: Make a modest wall + real actions behind it.
choices.B: Build the giant wall: powerful symbol.
choices.C: No wall, just publish a concrete plan.

mcc_143.title: Social media intern
mcc_143.body: An official post goes out with a strange emoji. Nobody knows what it means, but it is trending.

choices.A: Clarify and take back editorial control.
choices.B: Ride it: more emojis, more media noise.
choices.C: Leave it, but frame it with a charter.

mcc_144.title: Employee of the month
mcc_144.body: An internal "Employee of the Month" award comes back. Some love it, others call it toxic competition.

choices.A: Switch to a team reward, not individual.
choices.C: Keep it, but with collaboration criteria.

mcc_145.title: Compliance test
mcc_145.body: A compliance quiz is launched. 40% fail... and ask whether it "was graded."

choices.A: Train seriously, without punishment, and retest.
choices.C: Train + make the quiz simpler and more frequent.

mcc_146.title: Strategy on a napkin
mcc_146.body: An executive shows up with a strategy written on a napkin.

choices.A: Demand a structured plan before acting.
choices.C: Test it on a small scope, then decide.

mcc_147.title: Visitor badge
mcc_147.body: Visitor badges have become so strict that even you struggle to enter certain areas.

choices.A: Simplify things while keeping security.
choices.C: Keep it, but create CEO/emergency access.

mcc_148.title: Optimism week
mcc_148.body: HR launches an optimism week. Employees ask first: "can we sleep?"

choices.A: Tie it to a real workload reduction.
choices.B: Run the event without changing anything.
choices.C: Test it + anonymous feedback + adjust.

mcc_149.title: Product sold out
mcc_149.body: A meme makes your product go viral. Sold out everywhere. Investors laugh, logistics cries.

choices.A: Ramp up production, expensive, to catch the wave.
choices.C: Launch a transparent, staggered pre-order.

mcc_150.title: Presentation that is too long
mcc_150.body: Your presentation lasts 90 minutes. By the end, almost everyone has stopped paying attention.

choices.A: Shorten it and get to the point next time.
choices.B: Keep going and say that this format is necessary
choices.C: Keep a long presentation, but add concrete examples

mcc_151.title: Raw material shortage
mcc_151.body: A key material becomes rare and expensive. Your costs rise, your customers want stable pricing.

choices.A: Absorb part of the cost to protect customers.
choices.C: Look for alternatives and redesign the products.

mcc_152.title: Heat wave at a site
mcc_152.body: A heat wave makes work difficult at a logistics site. Productivity is down, fatigue is up.

choices.A: Slow the pace and increase breaks.
choices.C: Emergency investment, AC, adjusted hours, shuttles.

mcc_153.title: Energy going wild
mcc_153.body: The energy bill is exploding. The CFO wants cuts, the brand wants a clean transition.

choices.A: Accelerate energy savings and efficiency.
choices.C: Sign a more stable long-term contract, even if it costs more at first.

mcc_154.title: Packaging and plastic
mcc_154.body: Your packaging is criticized for too much plastic. Your teams say changing it is expensive and takes time.

choices.A: Move to cleaner packaging gradually.
choices.C: Launch an "eco" line as a test.

mcc_155.title: Strike at a subcontractor
mcc_155.body: A critical subcontractor is on strike. You are not responsible... but you are affected.

choices.A: Help mediate and support a solution.
choices.B: Switch subcontractors as fast as possible.
choices.C: Temporarily reduce production and communicate.

mcc_156.title: New data rule
mcc_156.body: A new rule requires more transparency on data. Your teams say: "this is heavy."

choices.A: Become as compliant as possible, fast.
choices.C: Turn it into an advantage: transparency + user control.

mcc_157.title: Global transport disrupted
mcc_157.body: Global transport slows down. Delays get longer, customers grow impatient.

choices.A: Increase local inventory for security.
choices.B: Keep going as before and handle it case by case.
choices.C: Rethink the supply chain: closer, more flexible.

mcc_158.title: Inflation and purchasing power
mcc_158.body: With inflation, your customers compare every dollar. Your product is seen as "expensive."

choices.A: Create a more affordable offer.
choices.B: Hold the line: quality and margin.
choices.C: Offer targeted temporary promos.

mcc_159.title: Report on your factories
mcc_159.body: A report wants to film one of your factories. Great exposure if all goes well, otherwise...

choices.A: Open the doors with transparency.
choices.C: Accept, but at an exemplary "pilot" site.

mcc_160.title: Leaked budget plan
mcc_160.body: An internal document about planned savings leaks out. Employees see it as a threat.

choices.A: Explain clearly + protect jobs as much as possible.
choices.B: Stay silent and wait for it to pass.
choices.C: Replace some cuts with a productivity plan.

mcc_161.title: New market, new culture
mcc_161.body: You are opening in a new country. A global campaign may land badly locally.

choices.A: Adapt the campaign with local teams.
choices.C: Run a neutral and cautious campaign.

mcc_162.title: Doubts about a supplier
mcc_162.body: A supplier is accused of questionable practices. Nothing is proven, but the noise is growing.

choices.A: Audit them and demand guarantees.
choices.C: Wait for confirmation before acting.

mcc_163.title: Greenwashing?
mcc_163.body: You are accused of "green marketing" without enough concrete action. The brand takes a hit.

choices.A: Publish proof and a quantified plan.
choices.C: Slow down the green messaging and speed up the actions.

mcc_164.title: Carbon and travel
mcc_164.body: Business travel is expensive and polluting. But some managers want to "see the teams."

choices.A: Limit travel, prioritize what is necessary.
choices.C: Create a quota + criteria, impact, usefulness.

mcc_165.title: Minor cyberattack
mcc_165.body: An attack disrupts one service for a few hours. Not a disaster, but it worries people.

choices.A: Strengthen security and be transparent after the incident.
choices.C: Strengthen security without communicating much.

mcc_166.title: Geopolitical tension
mcc_166.body: An area is becoming unstable, and your supply routes pass through it. The situation is directly threatening deliveries.

choices.A: Diversify routes and suppliers.
choices.C: Reduce sales that depend on that area.

mcc_167.title: Call for a boycott
mcc_167.body: A call for a boycott is spreading, with no clear reason at first. Mostly a wave on social media.

choices.A: Respond calmly with facts.
choices.B: Ignore it so you do not amplify it.
choices.C: Make a symbolic gesture, donation/initiative, to ease tensions.

mcc_168.title: Regulator in control mode
mcc_168.body: Regulators announce a series of sector-wide inspections. Your sector is on the list.

choices.A: Prepare a full internal audit.
choices.B: Do the bare minimum and cross your fingers.
choices.C: Cooperate publicly: "we have nothing to hide."

mcc_169.title: Circular economy
mcc_169.body: You are being pushed to take back and recycle your products.

choices.A: Launch a trade-in and repair program.
choices.C: Run a pilot on just one product line.

mcc_170.title: Hybrid work contested
mcc_170.body: Some teams want more remote work, others want more in-person time. The debate is heating up.

choices.A: Flexible team-based policy, with clear rules.
choices.C: Mandatory mostly remote work.

choices.A: Allow it with rules and approved tools.
choices.C: Allow it only for non-sensitive uses.

mcc_172.title: Advertising too targeted
mcc_172.body: People accuse you of overly targeted ads. Customers find it "intrusive."

choices.A: Reduce targeting and give users more control.
choices.C: Keep it, but make targeting transparent.

mcc_173.title: Water shortage
mcc_173.body: An industrial site faces water restrictions. You need to adapt quickly.

choices.A: Invest in water reduction and recycling.
choices.C: Maintain operations and buy more expensive water.

mcc_174.title: Banks more cautious
mcc_174.body: Banks are becoming more cautious about financing. Your expansion project is slowing down.

choices.A: Slow expansion and secure cash flow.
choices.B: Look for other financing, partners, market options.
choices.C: Keep going no matter what.

mcc_175.title: Multipolar world
mcc_175.body: Your international teams want more autonomy. Headquarters wants one global line.

choices.A: Give more local autonomy.
choices.C: Limited autonomy with global standards.

mcc_176.title: Payment outage
mcc_176.body: An outside payment company goes down. Sales stop cold for 2 hours.

choices.A: Add a second backup payment company.
choices.C: Create an "order reserved" mode for outages.

mcc_177.title: Eco-score on your products
mcc_177.body: An eco-score becomes visible. Your products are "average." Not disastrous, but nothing to be proud of.

choices.A: Improve materials and publish a roadmap.
choices.B: Ignore it: it is just a score.
choices.C: Improve one flagship line first.

mcc_178.title: Market slowdown
mcc_178.body: The market is slowing down. Investors want margins, employees want security.

choices.A: Freeze some spending, protect jobs.
choices.B: Cut hard to reassure the market.
choices.C: Invest in innovation to bounce back.

mcc_179.title: Trade tensions
mcc_179.body: Taxes appear between two regions. Your products cross the border every day.

choices.A: Reorganize production closer to customers.
choices.B: Pay and pass part of the cost on through prices.
choices.C: Negotiate and seek exemptions.

mcc_180.title: Social media and reputation
mcc_180.body: A small criticism goes viral. Nothing dramatic, but reputation moves fast.

choices.A: Respond quickly, humbly, and fix it.
choices.C: Run a positive campaign to drown out the noise.

mcc_181.title: Internal climate audit
mcc_181.body: Your internal committee proposes a climate audit of activities.

choices.A: Do the audit and publish realistic targets.
choices.C: Do the audit, but without external publication.

mcc_182.title: Local sourcing
mcc_182.body: You are being pushed to "buy local." It is good for your image, but sometimes more expensive.

choices.A: Increase the local share gradually.
choices.B: Stay with the cheapest global option.
choices.C: Go local only on certain key products.

mcc_183.title: Regulation on ads to children
mcc_183.body: A new rule limits advertising to certain audiences. Your ad team needs to adapt fast.

choices.A: Get compliant and rethink the strategy.
choices.C: Reduce advertising and lean on product quality.

mcc_184.title: Local housing crisis
mcc_184.body: In a city where you employ many people, housing becomes too expensive. Hiring gets harder.

choices.A: Housing/transport aid + local partnership.
choices.B: Ignore it: it is not your role.
choices.C: Move part of the teams elsewhere.

mcc_185.title: Green production chain
mcc_185.body: A supplier offers a greener option, but it is more expensive and slower.

choices.A: Choose the green option.
choices.C: Use a mix: green for part of the volume.

mcc_186.title: Executive pay
mcc_186.body: Your executive package is being criticized. No scandal, but a public debate over fairness.

choices.A: Reduce it / tie it more strongly to long-term goals.
choices.C: Make everything more transparent.

mcc_187.title: Global platform
mcc_187.body: Your service has to handle different rules by country. Product wants "one single version."

choices.A: Adapt by country, compliance first.
choices.B: One single version, even if it limits you in some countries.
choices.C: Global version + local modules.

mcc_188.title: Risk of disinformation
mcc_188.body: A fake article about your company is circulating. It is clearly exaggerated, but it is everywhere.

choices.A: Simple denial + proof + calm tone.
choices.B: Ignore it: the more you respond, the more it grows.
choices.C: Denial + minimal legal action.

mcc_189.title: Robots and jobs
mcc_189.body: Automating one site would save a lot. Employees fear for their jobs.

choices.A: Automate with a reskilling and mobility plan.
choices.C: Postpone automation.

mcc_190.title: Repairability
mcc_190.body: People are asking for more repairable products. It extends research time, but builds trust.

choices.A: Invest in repairability and support.
choices.B: Do not move: cost too high.
choices.C: Make premium products repairable first.

mcc_191.title: Multiple suppliers
mcc_191.body: Dependence on a single supplier is worrying people. A second supplier would cost more... but would make things safer.

choices.A: Add a second supplier.
choices.C: Second supplier only during peak periods.

mcc_192.title: Climate event and delays
mcc_192.body: A climate event blocks a logistics route. Deliveries will be late, for sure.

choices.A: Warn customers and compensate smartly.
choices.B: Say nothing as long as possible.
choices.C: Reroute at all costs, more expensive.

mcc_193.title: Risk of food shortages
mcc_193.body: In an area where you have employees, food prices are rising sharply. Morale is dropping.

choices.A: Short-term aid, cafeteria, meal vouchers.
choices.C: Negotiate discounted local partnerships.

mcc_194.title: Lobbying or education
mcc_194.body: A reform affects you. Some want "lobbying," others prefer "public education."

choices.A: Transparent official dialogue with institutions.
choices.C: Wait and adapt afterward.

mcc_195.title: Currencies and exchange rates
mcc_195.body: Exchange rates are moving sharply. Your international profits are becoming unpredictable.

choices.A: Hedge the risk and stabilize things.
choices.C: Reduce exposure by localizing more.

mcc_196.title: Pressure on deadlines
mcc_196.body: Customers want things "faster and faster." Employees say it is too much.

choices.A: Prioritize safety and quality, realistic deadlines.
choices.C: Create a cheaper "slow delivery" option.

mcc_197.title: Local political confusion
mcc_197.body: In one country, a political decision changes the rules overnight.

choices.A: Strengthen the local compliance team and adapt.
choices.C: Keep going and hope for clarification.

mcc_198.title: Education and talent
mcc_198.body: You are short on talent in key roles. The country lacks suitable training programs.

choices.A: Create an internal academy + school partnerships.
choices.C: Train internally, but slowly.

mcc_199.title: Results vs impact
mcc_199.body: The board wants better results. The ESG teams want greater social and environmental impact.

choices.A: Mixed goals: results + measurable impact.
choices.C: Strong impact now, even if margins fall.

mcc_200.title: Resilience plan
mcc_200.body: You are being asked for a resilience plan, crises, energy, supply. It takes time, but it reassures people.

choices.A: Build a full plan with scenarios.
choices.B: Make a minimal plan just to check the box.
choices.C: Make a plan, but test it at one pilot site.

mcc_201.title: Surprise ministerial visit
mcc_201.body: A minister announces a visit "to highlight industry."

choices.A: Play it with transparency: real visit, no set dressing.
choices.C: Offer a video conference: less stress, more control.

mcc_202.title: Anti-jargon law, almost
mcc_202.body: A lawmaker proposes a bill to limit jargon in contracts. Your lawyers politely faint.

choices.A: Simplify your contracts voluntarily.
choices.C: Create a clear summary in addition to the contract.

mcc_203.title: Parliamentary inquiry
mcc_203.body: A national commission wants to understand why "meetings last so long." You feel targeted.

choices.A: Launch an internal short-meeting reform.
choices.C: Sponsor a study... with your logo everywhere.

mcc_204.title: Green subsidy, paperwork
mcc_204.body: There is aid available to make one site greener, but the application is 84 pages long. The CFO sighs.

choices.A: Build the application properly and invest.
choices.C: Do a smaller pilot, simpler application.

mcc_205.title: Public committee of committees
mcc_205.body: Authorities create a committee about your sector.

choices.A: Participate actively and stay cooperative.
choices.C: Propose a simple charter to speed things up.

mcc_206.title: Price display outage
mcc_206.body: A bug shows inconsistent prices. A local official tweets: "So this is the high cost of living?"

choices.A: Fix it and communicate humbly.
choices.C: Offer something to affected customers.

mcc_207.title: Energy moderation week
mcc_207.body: The government launches an energy moderation week. Your offices are still far too brightly lit.

choices.A: Reduce lighting and show concrete actions.
choices.B: Change nothing: "we pay, so we light."
choices.C: Do a symbolic one-day action + long-term plan.

mcc_208.title: Surprise regulator audit
mcc_208.body: An email arrives: "on-site inspection tomorrow." Your compliance team is running everywhere.

choices.A: Fully cooperate and fix everything missing.
choices.C: Request an official delay and launch a quick internal audit.

mcc_209.title: Citizen business label
mcc_209.body: A city hall offers you a "citizen" badge if you fund a local event.

choices.A: Support the event, without overdoing it.
choices.B: Decline: no mixing business activity and local politics.
choices.C: Support it, but demand full transparency.

mcc_210.title: Speech at the economic forum
mcc_210.body: You have to speak at the economic forum. Two minutes. Your communications team wrote... 12 minutes.

choices.A: Cut it and keep it simple, direct.
choices.B: Read it all: "vision has no time limit."
choices.C: Do 2 minutes + an optional written note.

mcc_211.title: Campaign too forceful
mcc_211.body: The ad team proposes "Made here, with pride." In some countries, that rubs people the wrong way.

choices.A: Adapt by country to avoid misunderstandings.
choices.C: Make the message neutral: quality over flag.

mcc_212.title: Online public consultation
mcc_212.body: A public consultation on your sector is open. Comments range from "great" to "stop everything."

choices.A: Respond calmly and propose commitments.
choices.C: Have a professional association answer for the sector.

mcc_213.title: Debate on advertising
mcc_213.body: A proposed law limits certain ads.

choices.A: Adapt and lean on transparency.
choices.B: Test the edge of the framework.
choices.C: Reduce advertising and rely on the product.

mcc_214.title: Networking dinner, very networking
mcc_214.body: You are invited to a dinner where everyone is on a first-name basis... and nobody clearly says why.

choices.A: Go, but stay fully transparent about your conversations.
choices.C: Go with a compliance team and a clear agenda.

mcc_215.title: Surprise tax
mcc_215.body: A sector tax is announced. Your investors grumble, your customers do not want to pay more.

choices.A: Absorb part of it and optimize costs.
choices.B: Pass part of the cost on through prices.
choices.C: Adapt the offer, entry-level option to limit the impact.

mcc_216.title: Pressure for local
mcc_216.body: An elected official asks you: "Why not 100% local?" Your supply chain is under pressure.

choices.A: Increase local sourcing gradually with targets.
choices.C: Explain the reality and propose a mix.

mcc_217.title: Demonstration in front of headquarters
mcc_217.body: A small protest appears in front of your headquarters with creative signs. Very creative.

choices.A: Open a calm dialogue and listen to the demands.
choices.B: Secure the area and ignore it.
choices.C: Offer an official meeting with mediation.

mcc_218.title: Rumor of a big secret plan
mcc_218.body: Social media is going wild: supposedly you have a "secret plan" with the government. You do not even know about it.

choices.A: Deny it simply, with facts.
choices.B: Let it run: "it will seem mysterious."
choices.C: Publish a transparency update, FAQ.

mcc_219.title: Election day
mcc_219.body: The country is voting. Your teams ask whether sensitive topics can be avoided at work. Good idea.

choices.A: Reminder of respect + neutrality at work.
choices.C: Organize a calm moment, break, listening, mediation.

mcc_220.title: Repairability law is coming
mcc_220.body: A law requires more repairability. Your engineers say: "We can... but not by tomorrow morning."

choices.A: Invest and get ahead of it, with a clear plan.
choices.C: Start with the flagship products.

mcc_221.title: Intern parliament
mcc_221.body: Interns organize an "internal parliament" to propose ideas.

choices.A: Listen and keep the best ideas.
choices.B: Stop it: "we are not a republic."
choices.C: Keep it, but frame it, 1 topic per month.

mcc_222.title: Logo diplomatic crisis
mcc_222.body: Your logo resembles an official symbol in one country. Nobody checked. Oops.

choices.A: Adapt the logo locally.
choices.C: Change it globally, major project.

mcc_223.title: Mayor wants a word
mcc_223.body: The mayor of the city where you have a site wants "a word." Your team guesses: jobs, subsidies, photo op...

choices.A: Meet, talk jobs and concrete projects.
choices.C: Send a representative + measurable commitments.

mcc_224.title: Debate on fair prices
mcc_224.body: A TV show debates "fair prices" and names your company. Your communications team is smiling through gritted teeth.

choices.A: Explain your cost structure clearly and calmly.
choices.C: Make a gesture on one popular product line.

mcc_225.title: National social barometer
mcc_225.body: A social barometer compares companies. You are "average." The worst part: it is public.

choices.A: Launch a wellness and improvement plan.
choices.B: Run a communications operation to climb quickly.
choices.C: Publicly challenge the barometer and its method.

mcc_226.title: Decarbonization in 3 slides
mcc_226.body: You are asked for a decarbonization plan in 3 slides. The board wants it short. The topic needs substance.

choices.A: Simple but serious plan with quantified steps.
choices.B: 3 vague slides, and we will see later.
choices.C: Long internal plan + short public version.

mcc_227.title: Conference on the world after
mcc_227.body: You are invited to speak about the "world after." Nobody knows what that means, but everyone applauds.

choices.A: Speak concretely: actions, targets, limits.
choices.B: Give an inspiring speech with no details.
choices.C: Decline and publish a clear note instead.

mcc_228.title: AI regulation
mcc_228.body: A regulation on AI is coming.

choices.A: Frame AI: allowed uses + audit.
choices.C: Allow it internally only, not for customers.

mcc_229.title: Consumer protection
mcc_229.body: A new rule requires more clarity on subscriptions.

choices.A: Make everything clear and easy to cancel.
choices.C: Improve things, without changing everything.

mcc_230.title: Debated public holiday
mcc_230.body: A national debate over a public holiday is coming.

choices.A: Keep the day and compensate with organization.
choices.B: Remove the day in the company.
choices.C: Keep it, but make it optional by role, with compensation.

mcc_231.title: Possible public contract
mcc_231.body: A major public contract is up for grabs. Great visibility, but strict compliance requirements.

choices.A: Go for it: exemplary compliance.
choices.C: Go for it through an experienced partner.

mcc_232.title: Political debate on wages
mcc_232.body: The debate over wages is exploding. Your employees want a gesture. The CFO wants calm.

choices.A: Targeted raises + transparency.
choices.C: One-time bonus + long-term plan.

mcc_233.title: Long non-financial report
mcc_233.body: Your non-financial report is 120 pages long.

choices.A: Shorten it and make it concrete, with indicators.
choices.C: Keep it long + mandatory 1-page summary.

mcc_234.title: Advertising and neutrality
mcc_234.body: One ad is judged "too political" by some, "not committed enough" by others. Impressive.

choices.A: Pull it and redo it in a more neutral way.
choices.C: Keep it, but clarify the intent.

mcc_235.title: Label review
mcc_235.body: A "responsible" label is now being checked more strictly. Your teams ask: "Did we really earn it?"

choices.A: Audit and fix things before the inspection.
choices.C: Temporarily remove the label to stay cautious.

mcc_236.title: Cafeteria vote
mcc_236.body: Employees vote for more plant-based food in the cafeteria. Some are shouting about a broccoli conspiracy.

choices.A: Add more plant-based options, without forcing it.
choices.B: Refuse: "the cafeteria is not a parliament."
choices.C: Test 2 days a week and measure satisfaction.

mcc_237.title: Media trust crisis
mcc_237.body: The media is talking a lot about your sector.

choices.A: Proactive transparency and proof.
choices.C: Speak only through one spokesperson.

mcc_238.title: Debate on local data
mcc_238.body: You are being asked whether your data should stay "in the country." Tech says yes, finance says "that costs money."

choices.A: Localize sensitive data.
choices.C: Hybrid solution: sensitive data local, the rest global.

mcc_239.title: Anti-waste law
mcc_239.body: An anti-waste law is coming. Your unsold inventory is staring at you... sadly.

choices.A: Donate/recycle it and rethink production.
choices.C: Sell it at a discount through a dedicated platform.

mcc_240.title: Origin label
mcc_240.body: A rule requires more clarity on origin. Your packaging is going to look like a passport.

choices.A: Show the origin clearly and own it.
choices.B: Do the bare minimum in tiny print.
choices.C: Show the origin + a clear explanation.

mcc_241.title: Debate on profits
mcc_241.body: Your company is making good profits.

choices.A: Invest more locally: jobs, training.
choices.C: Create a modest social fund + low-key communication.

mcc_242.title: Subcontractor oversight
mcc_242.body: A new rule makes you more responsible for your subcontractors.

choices.A: Audit them seriously and require standards.
choices.B: Switch subcontractors quickly, without checking much.
choices.C: Audit only the most critical ones.

mcc_243.title: Demonstration... to congratulate you
mcc_243.body: An association publicly congratulates you for a green initiative.

choices.A: Thank them humbly and keep going.
choices.B: Turn it into a major ad campaign.
choices.C: Stay discreet to avoid backlash.

mcc_244.title: Debate on schedules
mcc_244.body: A national reform on work schedules is being discussed.

choices.A: Adapt schedules through consultation.
choices.B: Push to keep the current model.
choices.C: Automate part of the work and reduce the toughest hours.

mcc_245.title: Carbon neutrality conference
mcc_245.body: You promise "carbon neutrality" on stage. Your operations lead just texts you: "😅".

choices.A: Reframe it: realistic goals, clear steps.
choices.B: Keep the promise and accelerate at all costs.
choices.C: Make the promise for one product line/region first.

mcc_246.title: New anti-fraud rule
mcc_246.body: An anti-fraud rule requires more checks. Your product teams say: "that will add 3 clicks."

choices.A: Strengthen checks while keeping the experience simple.
choices.C: Progressive checks based on risk.

mcc_247.title: Monopoly debate
mcc_247.body: Big companies are accused of dominating too much. You are invited to "justify your size." That is new.

choices.A: Show your contribution: jobs, innovation, prices.
choices.C: Announce pro-competition commitments: APIs, partnerships.

mcc_248.title: National car-free day
mcc_248.body: The country is holding a car-free day.

choices.A: Remote work that day + alternative transport.
choices.C: Partial attendance + staggered schedules.

mcc_249.title: Debate on online content
mcc_249.body: A law wants to regulate certain online content. Your service has moderation... "optimistic" moderation.

choices.A: Strengthen moderation and transparency.
choices.C: Strengthen it only for critical cases.

mcc_250.title: Big globalization debate
mcc_250.body: On TV, people are debating globalization.

choices.A: Calmly explain your value chain and local efforts.
choices.B: Stay silent: the less said, the better.
choices.C: Run a mini educational campaign: "how it works."

mcc_251.title: Debate on video meetings
mcc_251.body: A political party proposes limiting meetings to 30 minutes "to save the country's productivity."

choices.A: Adopt the rule internally and publish the results.
choices.B: Refuse: "a meeting is something you experience."
choices.C: Do 30 minutes... with an official "10-minute bonus."

mcc_252.title: Senator and the coffee machine
mcc_252.body: A senator visits your headquarters and falls in love with your coffee machine. He wants to showcase it in session.

choices.A: Accept: a nice little spotlight.
choices.C: Donate a machine... to a public training center.

mcc_253.title: Zero weekend email strike
mcc_253.body: An internal movement demands zero emails on weekends. An elected official applauds.

choices.A: Put in a strict charter and clear rules.
choices.C: Test it for 2 months and measure the impact.

mcc_254.title: Debate on indecent profits
mcc_254.body: During a televised debate, your company is cited as an example. The tone used makes your brand look ridiculous in front of everyone.

choices.A: Answer with numbers + investments + jobs.
choices.B: Ignore it: speaking feeds the controversy.
choices.C: Announce a visible, concrete training/jobs plan.

mcc_255.title: Official photo
mcc_255.body: You are offered a photo with a minister in front of your factory. Your teams wonder how far the staging should go.

choices.A: Accept, but stay sober and factual.
choices.C: Accept only if you announce a real and useful training project.

mcc_256.title: Anti-bureaucracy committee
mcc_256.body: A public committee wants to reduce bureaucracy. It asks for... a 60-page report.

choices.A: Play along and propose concrete simplifications.
choices.C: Propose a 1-page format + annexed data.

mcc_257.title: Mayor wants a promise
mcc_257.body: The mayor wants a jobs promise for the next election.

choices.A: Measured promise: goals + conditions.
choices.C: Propose a training plan that creates indirect jobs.

mcc_258.title: Industrial sovereignty
mcc_258.body: You are being asked to relocate production "fast." Your factories reply: "fast means 24 months."

choices.A: Relocate a strategic part, step by step.
choices.C: Relocate only final assembly.

mcc_259.title: Salary clarity law
mcc_259.body: A law strengthens pay transparency. HR says: "We are going to get... emails."

choices.A: Anticipate it: clear pay bands + explanations.
choices.C: Publish only broad salary ranges.

mcc_260.title: Lawmaker and the packaging
mcc_260.body: A lawmaker holds up your packaging in Parliament: "Look at this plastic!" Your engineers sigh loudly.

choices.A: Speed up the packaging transition and say it clearly.
choices.B: Answer: "it is for safety and hygiene."
choices.C: Launch a zero-plastic pilot on one product line.

mcc_261.title: Debate on over-the-top ads
mcc_261.body: A proposed law targets ads that are too intrusive. Your ad team asks whether a screaming pop-up counts.

choices.A: Move to softer, clearer advertising.
choices.C: Keep it, but reduce the most aggressive formats.

mcc_262.title: Roundtable at the Assembly
mcc_262.body: You are invited to a roundtable. All the other guests already have opinions... about you.

choices.A: Go, stay factual, and propose commitments.
choices.C: Go with a delegation: compliance + HR + ESG.

mcc_263.title: Contract and favoritism rumor
mcc_263.body: A rumor says you got a public contract thanks to "friends." It is false, but it is spreading.

choices.A: Publish the process and selection criteria.
choices.C: Request an independent external audit.

mcc_264.title: Exemplary business label
mcc_264.body: A public label offers an “exemplary” badge. Your teams fear it may look like a decorative reward.

choices.A: Accept it if the criteria are strict and auditable.
choices.C: Accept it, but do not promote it.

mcc_265.title: Debate on water
mcc_265.body: A water crisis is coming in a region where you produce. An elected official wants an "immediate" plan.

choices.A: Invest in water recycling and reduction.
choices.C: Keep going and pay more for access.

choices.A: Reform: fewer meetings, more autonomy.
choices.B: Crack down: "we do not negotiate with calendars."
choices.C: Create a quota: limited meetings, justified exceptions.

mcc_267.title: Debate on the carbon tax
mcc_267.body: A carbon tax is being discussed. Investors want margins. Authorities want seriousness.

choices.A: Speed up the transition + public quantified plan.
choices.C: Pass part of the cost on through prices.

mcc_268.title: Call to consume less
mcc_268.body: A public campaign pushes people to consume less. Your product is... exactly a consumer product.

choices.A: Focus on durability, repair, second life.
choices.C: Shift toward responsible services/subscriptions.

mcc_269.title: Debate on youth employment
mcc_269.body: A minister announces a youth employment plan. Your HR team opens its spreadsheet.

choices.A: Launch a major apprenticeship program.
choices.C: Train fewer young people, but better, quality first.

mcc_270.title: Stronger anti-fraud controls
mcc_270.body: The regulator wants more controls on your sales. Product fears a heavier customer journey.

choices.A: Strengthen controls while simplifying the experience.
choices.C: Progressive controls based on risk level.

mcc_271.title: Debate on dividends
mcc_271.body: A political debate criticizes high dividends. Your shareholders love them. Your employees... less so.

choices.A: Lower dividends, invest more, and share internally.
choices.C: Maintain them, but create a performance bonus for teams.

mcc_272.title: No-AC day
mcc_272.body: A public campaign proposes a day without AC. Your offices say: "We are going to melt."

choices.A: Take part: adjust temperature + communicate.
choices.C: Take part only on certain sites.

mcc_273.title: Digital sovereignty
mcc_273.body: You are being asked to store data locally. Tech says yes, finance says "ouch."

choices.A: Localize sensitive data and audit the rest.
choices.C: Hybrid approach: local + multi-region cloud.

mcc_274.title: Anti-inflation pact
mcc_274.body: The government proposes a pact: some prices capped. Your margins are afraid.

choices.A: Join the pact on a few products.
choices.C: Join, but with trade-offs, volume, duration.

mcc_275.title: Lawmaker wants a simulator
mcc_275.body: A lawmaker wants a simulator to explain your pricing to the public. Your CFO is grimacing.

choices.A: Make a simple educational simulator.
choices.C: Create an FAQ with 3 key numbers.

mcc_276.title: International summit photo
mcc_276.body: International summit: you are placed in an official photo. You are between two very serious people.

choices.A: Own it, stay neutral and factual.
choices.C: Accept, but publish your message: economy + jobs + climate.

mcc_277.title: Debate on the 4-day week
mcc_277.body: The 4-day week is back in public debate. Employees applaud. Finance sweats.

choices.A: Test it in some departments with clear targets.
choices.C: Offer flexibility, compressed days.

mcc_278.title: Anti-lobbying charter
mcc_278.body: A public charter asks companies to disclose their exchanges.

choices.A: Sign it and publish your key exchanges.
choices.C: Sign it, but limit exchanges to official channels only.

mcc_279.title: Debate on toxic content
mcc_279.body: A political debate calls for more online moderation.

choices.A: Strengthen moderation + transparency.
choices.C: Strengthen it only for serious cases.

mcc_280.title: Debate on mega-companies
mcc_280.body: A politician says: "the giants need to be broken up."

choices.A: Propose pro-competition commitments, partnerships, APIs.
choices.C: Voluntarily split off one business line, symbolically.

mcc_281.title: Product recall alert
mcc_281.body: An authority flags a minor risk on one batch. It is not serious, but the headline could hurt.

choices.A: Recall the batch and communicate clearly.
choices.C: Fix it quietly and monitor.

mcc_282.title: A trap interview
mcc_282.body: A journalist asks you: "Are you more about profit or the planet?" It is a trap, and they know it.

choices.A: Answer: mixed goals + proof.
choices.C: Have your ESG lead answer with numbers.

mcc_283.title: Debate on immigration
mcc_283.body: The country is debating talent visas. Your tech teams are short on people, and your image is sensitive.

choices.A: Support a balanced training + hiring program.
choices.C: Recruit abroad without communicating about it.

mcc_284.title: National austerity plan
mcc_284.body: The government is cutting some budgets.

choices.A: Diversify clients and secure jobs.
choices.C: Invest in a new, more profitable product.

mcc_285.title: Debate on gifts
mcc_285.body: A rule limits gifts to decision-makers.

choices.A: Zero gifts, strict compliance.
choices.C: Replace gifts with public, transparent events.

mcc_286.title: Big digital reduction plan
mcc_286.body: Companies are being asked to reduce digital consumption.

choices.A: Optimize, reduce, measure, and publish.
choices.C: Optimize only the most resource-heavy services.

mcc_287.title: Debate on hidden margins
mcc_287.body: An elected official claims you have hidden margins. Your CFO says: "if only..."

choices.A: Open your costs on one product line, transparency.
choices.C: Publish simple indicators, average margin, investments.

mcc_288.title: Internal vote on ethics charter
mcc_288.body: Employees want to vote on an ethics charter.

choices.A: Adopt the charter and make it operational.
choices.C: Adopt a short charter + monitoring committee.

mcc_289.title: Anti-offshoring plan
mcc_289.body: A government launches an anti-offshoring plan. Your costs go up, and so does your image.

choices.A: Partially relocate, strategically.
choices.C: Relocate only the visible final stage.

choices.A: Explain costs + propose a cheaper basic option.
choices.C: Propose a temporary price freeze on one product line.

mcc_291.title: Debate on crisis profits
mcc_291.body: In a tense period, some are accused of "crisis profits."

choices.A: Publish an anti-abuse commitment + internal controls.
choices.C: Create a support fund for vulnerable customers.

mcc_292.title: Transparency day
mcc_292.body: A national transparency day is launched.

choices.A: Publish key data on prices, impacts, and salary ranges.
choices.C: Publish only what is required.

mcc_293.title: Delivery footprint
mcc_293.body: Fast deliveries are being criticized. Your customers love them. The planet, less so.

choices.A: Offer a slower "green" option by default.
choices.C: Invest in cleaner fleets, gradually.

mcc_294.title: Anti-advertising debate
mcc_294.body: A reform aims to reduce advertising. Your ad team panics: "what about our jingles?"

choices.A: Cut ads and strengthen word of mouth/quality.
choices.B: Keep advertising and work around it through influencers.
choices.C: Adapt: fewer ads, more useful information.

mcc_295.title: Debate on algorithms
mcc_295.body: A law wants more algorithmic transparency.

choices.A: Transparency: principles + audits + user control.
choices.C: Partial transparency on sensitive points.

mcc_296.title: Official wants a citizen price
mcc_296.body: An elected official asks you for a lower "citizen price."

choices.A: Create a limited civic offer, with conditions.
choices.C: Create a coupon through public partners.

mcc_297.title: Debate on workplace safety
mcc_297.body: After a minor incident, public debate catches fire.

choices.A: Strengthen safety + training + transparency.
choices.C: Strengthen safety, but communicate only internally.

mcc_298.title: National anti-stress week
mcc_298.body: The government launches an anti-stress week.

choices.A: Reduce reporting + support, coaching, breaks.
choices.C: Do a one-time action + long-term HR plan.

mcc_299.title: Debate on energy prices
mcc_299.body: Energy prices are a major topic of debate.

choices.A: Invest in efficiency + clean energy.
choices.C: Sign a long-term contract + reduce consumption.

mcc_300.title: Nation vs world debate
mcc_300.body: On a TV set, someone asks: "Are you a national company or a global one?" You answer: "yes."

choices.A: Highlight local jobs + the global network.
choices.C: Present yourself as 100% local, good image, without too much detail.

mcc_301.title: The red button law
mcc_301.body: A lawmaker wants all services to have a red "Cancel in 1 click" button.

choices.A: Create a real red button, clear and immediate.
choices.B: Create a button... but hide it in advanced settings.
choices.C: Create a red button + a help flow that retains customers.

mcc_302.title: Speech on the value of work
mcc_302.body: A minister gives a speech about the value of work.

choices.A: Announce a simple performance-based bonus.
choices.B: Announce more targets, no more resources.
choices.C: Launch a training + internal mobility plan.

mcc_303.title: Anti-fake news commission
mcc_303.body: A commission asks how you fight disinformation.

choices.A: Answer seriously: transparency and moderation if needed.
choices.B: Reply with a joke, bad idea
choices.C: Propose a voluntary sector charter.

mcc_304.title: Debate on mega-bonuses
mcc_304.body: A proposed law wants to regulate executive bonuses.

choices.A: Reduce them and tie them to social/environmental goals.
choices.B: Get around it through indirect perks.
choices.C: Make pay transparent and justified.

mcc_305.title: Mayor wants a showcase factory
mcc_305.body: The mayor wants a showcase factory for his campaign. Your teams mostly want a factory that works.

choices.A: Create a public visitor path with real information.
choices.C: Offer a neutral open-house day, no slogans.

mcc_306.title: Debate on packaging
mcc_306.body: A law wants to limit packaging. Your product has packaging for the packaging. Yes.

choices.A: Cut it drastically and communicate proof.
choices.C: Switch to refill/reusable on one product line.

mcc_307.title: Patriotic ad plan
mcc_307.body: The ad team proposes a campaign with a flag, fanfare, and the slogan "At home with us!"

choices.A: Adapt it: value jobs without chauvinism.
choices.B: Keep the fanfare: it makes an impression.
choices.C: Remove the flag: keep only the quality message.

mcc_308.title: Debate on screen-free week
mcc_308.body: A screen-free week is being proposed.

choices.A: Offer a "moderation" mode with limited time.
choices.B: Ignore it and push notifications, ouch.
choices.C: Support the initiative and offer built-in breaks.

mcc_309.title: Audit of public contracts
mcc_309.body: An audit is reviewing public contracts in your sector. They want "everything," including emails from 2014.

choices.A: Fully cooperate, neatly and in order.
choices.B: Resist and provide the strict minimum.
choices.C: Request an external audit and deliver a clear file.

mcc_310.title: Debate on an ad tax
mcc_310.body: People are discussing an advertising tax. Your team is already trying to figure out what will be affected.

choices.A: Reduce advertising and strengthen quality/service.
choices.C: Diversify: useful ads + transparency + less intrusive formats.

mcc_311.title: Right to disconnect reform
mcc_311.body: The right to disconnect is being strengthened.

choices.A: Block sending emails at night, except emergencies.
choices.B: Let it happen: "passion has no schedule."
