choices.C: Allow sending, but automatically schedule delivery for the next day.

mcc_312.title: Debate on AI at work
mcc_312.body: A law wants to regulate AI at work. Your employees ask whether AI is also going to make the coffee.

choices.A: AI charter: uses, protections, training.
choices.C: Use AI only for non-sensitive internal tasks.

mcc_313.title: Visitor badge scandal
mcc_313.body: A lawmaker is outraged on social media.

choices.A: Strengthen security + apologies + public fixes.
choices.C: Officially invite the press, in a controlled way, and explain.

mcc_314.title: Debate on round prices
mcc_314.body: An elected official wants to ban prices ending in .99, calling them "manipulative." Your pricing policy is shaking.

choices.A: Switch to round prices + explain why.
choices.C: Test round prices on one product line only.

mcc_315.title: Photo with the candidate
mcc_315.body: A candidate wants a photo with you. He promises to "save the economy." Your comms team is screaming internally.

choices.A: Refuse: strict neutrality.
choices.C: Accept only a private meeting with no photo.

mcc_316.title: 100% local on a TV debate
mcc_316.body: A TV debate demands 100% local. Your supply chain says that is unrealistic.

choices.A: Progressive targets + public indicators.
choices.B: Promise 100% local and cross your fingers.
choices.C: Focus on traceability rather than slogans.

mcc_317.title: Clearer terms
mcc_317.body: Your terms of use are 40 pages long. Even your lawyer does not read them anymore.

choices.A: 1-page summary + icons + simple words.
choices.B: Leave it like that: it is legal.
choices.C: Simplify it, but keep the large paragraphs.

mcc_318.title: Debate on profits and water
mcc_318.body: One region is running out of water. Industries are being blamed. Your factory drinks... quite a lot.

choices.A: Invest in water recycling + measured reductions.
choices.B: Keep going and pay the extra cost.
choices.C: Move part of production.

mcc_319.title: Local cafeteria amendment
mcc_319.body: An amendment wants to make the cafeteria more local. Your employees mostly want it to taste good.

choices.A: More local + varied menu, with testing and feedback.
choices.C: Go local only for certain products.

mcc_320.title: Debate against hypergrowth
mcc_320.body: A political movement criticizes hypergrowth. Your investors love... hypergrowth.

choices.A: Refocus: growth + responsibility, with proof.
choices.C: Set boundaries: moderate growth, more stability.

mcc_321.title: Outage during the TV debate
mcc_321.body: Your service goes down right in the middle of a TV debate.

choices.A: Immediate transparency + simple technical report.
choices.B: Radio silence until it is fixed.
choices.C: Compensation + low-key communication.

mcc_322.title: Tax on private jets
mcc_322.body: A tax on private jets becomes a debate.

choices.A: Clarify: business travel + modest travel policy.
choices.C: Publish a sustainable mobility policy.

mcc_323.title: Debate on internships
mcc_323.body: A proposal wants to better regulate internships.

choices.A: Paid internships + real assignments + mentoring.
choices.C: Keep internships, but sharply limit their number.

mcc_324.title: Anti-greenwashing law
mcc_324.body: A law against image-only environmental claims is coming. The ad team has put "eco" everywhere.

choices.A: Clean up the messaging + proof + audits.
choices.C: Reduce promises, keep the actions.

mcc_325.title: Debate on remote work
mcc_325.body: Remote work is becoming a political topic.

choices.A: Clear and stable hybrid model.
choices.C: Broad remote work + performance rules.

mcc_326.title: Data security
mcc_326.body: After a highly publicized leak elsewhere, the regulator wants guarantees.

choices.A: Invest heavily + external audit + transparency.
choices.C: Invest gradually on the critical points.

mcc_327.title: Innovation subsidy
mcc_327.body: An innovation subsidy is possible, but you need to prove you are innovating.

choices.A: Real R&D project and strong application.
choices.B: Rename one button and submit without a real project.
choices.C: A modest but real pilot project.

choices.A: Ambitious apprenticeship program.
choices.C: Program targeted at hard-to-fill jobs.

mcc_329.title: The Cash & Clash show
mcc_329.body: A TV show wants to follow you for a week. Your communications team is panicking. Finance too.

choices.A: Accept with transparency, and preparation.
choices.C: Offer a guided visit + public documents.

mcc_330.title: Debate on anti-layoff law
mcc_330.body: A political party proposes to limit layoffs.

choices.A: Avoid layoffs: mobility + training.
choices.C: Freeze hiring and cut costs elsewhere.

mcc_331.title: Buy less campaign
mcc_331.body: A public campaign encourages people to buy less. Your ad team takes it as a personal attack.

choices.A: Offer repair, secondhand, durability.
choices.C: Launch a more durable line, higher price.

mcc_332.title: Debate on fast delivery
mcc_332.body: A lawmaker wants to limit express delivery for environmental reasons. Your customers wanted it yesterday.

choices.A: Slow option by default + paid express.
choices.C: Express only in limited areas + cleaner fleet.

mcc_333.title: A union invites an elected official
mcc_333.body: A union invites an elected official to talk at your site. Your managers ask: "and us, do we come too?"

choices.A: Three-way meeting: elected officials + employees + leadership.
choices.C: Accept, but in a public, structured format.

mcc_334.title: Debate on moderation
mcc_334.body: Your brand’s booth is far too overloaded. It gives an excessive image instead of a serious one.

choices.A: Real moderation plan + visible measures.
choices.C: Do nothing: productivity first.

mcc_335.title: Debate on commissions
mcc_335.body: If you do payments, people are asking you to lower commissions.

choices.A: Lower them + offset through volume/efficiency.
choices.C: Create a cheaper offer for small merchants.

mcc_336.title: Entry of a sovereign wealth fund
mcc_336.body: A sovereign wealth fund wants to invest.

choices.A: Accept with safeguards and transparency.
choices.C: Accept only a minority stake.

mcc_337.title: Debate on dynamic pricing
mcc_337.body: Dynamic pricing is under criticism. Your algorithm is proud. People, less so.

choices.A: Limit it and make it transparent, with caps.
choices.C: Keep it, but only on a few products.

mcc_338.title: Debate on air quality
mcc_338.body: A city imposes a stricter air standard.

choices.A: Invest in filters and modernization.
choices.C: Temporarily reduce production.

mcc_339.title: Debate on payment terms
mcc_339.body: A law wants to reduce payment delays to suppliers.

choices.A: Pay faster, secure the supply chain.
choices.B: Pay on the latest possible day.
choices.C: Pay small suppliers fast, normal terms for big ones.

mcc_340.title: Anti-open-space proposal
mcc_340.body: A political movement wants to limit "toxic" open-plan offices.

choices.A: Create more quiet areas and closed offices.
choices.C: Broader remote work to reduce open-space density.

mcc_341.title: A lawmaker criticizes your support
mcc_341.body: A lawmaker publicly complains about your customer service. Your customer service complains publicly... internally.

choices.A: Strengthen support + keep the promised response times.
choices.C: Offer a VIP channel for public cases, and improve the rest.

mcc_342.title: Debate on golden parachutes
mcc_342.body: Golden parachutes are back in the spotlight. Your board proposes a "beige parachute, more discreet."

choices.A: Reduce it and publish a reasonable policy.
choices.C: Tie it to long-term performance, not just departure.

mcc_343.title: No-surprise-bill law
mcc_343.body: A law wants to ban surprise fees. Your product has a small "service" fee. Very small... to you.

choices.A: Remove hidden fees, clear pricing.
choices.B: Keep it, in smaller print, no.
choices.C: Keep it, but make it ultra visible before payment.

mcc_344.title: Debate on training
mcc_344.body: A reform requires more training. HR says "great," your manager says "when?"

choices.A: Major training plan + dedicated time.
choices.C: Training focused on critical skills.

mcc_345.title: Internal elections
mcc_345.body: Internal elections are stirring up the company. Flyers are circulating. Even the coffee machine has an opinion.

choices.A: Make room for a healthy, neutral debate.
choices.C: Limit the internal campaign, too strictly.

mcc_346.title: Debate on product returns
mcc_346.body: People are criticizing the waste created by returns.

choices.A: Reduce returns through better info + reconditioning.
choices.C: Charge a small share of return costs.

choices.A: Cooperate: transparency + clear rules.
choices.C: Self-regulate before the law arrives.

mcc_348.title: Debate on commute time
mcc_348.body: A proposal wants part of commuting time counted as working time.

choices.A: Negotiate a fair internal framework.
choices.B: Refuse and wait for the law.
choices.C: Compensate with remote work and flexible hours.

mcc_349.title: A minister quotes your slogan
mcc_349.body: A minister quotes your slogan in a speech. He quotes it wrong. Very wrong. The internet loves it.

choices.A: React with humor + gentle correction.
choices.C: Say nothing: let it pass.

mcc_350.title: Debate on national productivity
mcc_350.body: National productivity is a hot topic.

choices.A: Productivity plan: tools + simplification + human respect.
choices.B: Productivity plan: pressure and tougher targets.
choices.C: Productivity plan: automation + training.

mcc_351.title: Unionized hamster
mcc_351.body: Your comms mascot, a hamster, is "elected" staff representative after a troll vote.

choices.A: Accept it: luxury wheel + serious statement, yikes.
choices.B: Refuse: "a hamster is not an employee."
choices.C: Create a wellness program... for humans, and the hamster too.

mcc_352.title: Absurd audit
mcc_352.body: A regulator asks for an audit on a ridiculous issue.

choices.A: Cooperate seriously despite the absurdity
choices.B: Make fun of it and ignore it, bad idea.
choices.C: Send a serious file + offer non-slip mats.

mcc_353.title: Coffee machine for office
mcc_353.body: Your coffee machine starts displaying: "Vote for me, I lower stress." The media loves it.

choices.A: Capitalize on it: a controlled humorous campaign.
choices.B: Turn off the machine: end the chaos, start the anger.
choices.C: Launch a real anti-stress action and calm the media noise.

mcc_354.title: Emoji quota
mcc_354.body: A lawmaker proposes limiting smileys at work. Your Slack becomes a disaster zone.

choices.A: Comply: clearer communication, fewer emojis.
choices.C: Create an "emoji weekend" mode, no smileys on weekdays.

mcc_355.title: Hologram meeting
mcc_355.body: A minister wants to meet you, but only as a hologram "for innovation."

choices.A: Do the hologram: wow effect guaranteed.
choices.C: Do a simple VR demo, no spectacle.

mcc_356.title: Logo blinking
mcc_356.body: Your new AI-generated logo starts blinking on its own on the site. Customers think it is alive.

choices.A: Own it: "living logo" and turn it into a mascot.
choices.B: Remove it right away: too weird.
choices.C: Keep it, but disable the blinking, moderation.

mcc_357.title: Transparency oath
mcc_357.body: A law requires CEOs to take a "transparency oath" on camera.

choices.A: Do it: clear words + verifiable commitments.
choices.C: Do it, but also publish a concrete report.

mcc_358.title: Delivery drone... at Parliament
mcc_358.body: Your delivery drone gets the address wrong and drops a package at Parliament.

choices.A: Apologize + logistics audit + humor.
choices.B: Say it was a "test," dangerous lie.
choices.C: Offer a demo of secure delivery to regulators.

mcc_359.title: Tax on pointless media noise
mcc_359.body: An elected official proposes a tax on pointless media noise. Your ad team panics.

choices.A: Reduce flashy campaigns, focus on usefulness.
choices.B: Double the media noise before the law arrives.
choices.C: Turn media noise into education, transparency, impact.

mcc_360.title: Press release in poetry
mcc_360.body: Your comms team wants to respond with a poem. Your CFO wants a chart.

choices.A: Chart + simple language, cautious.
choices.C: Poem + chart in the appendix.

mcc_361.title: No-CEO day
mcc_361.body: An internal movement launches a "No CEO Day."

choices.A: Play along: delegation, autonomy, trust.
choices.C: Accept it, but post a short written message.

choices.A: Go, stay humble, and show proof.
choices.C: Go, but announce a new concrete improvement.

mcc_363.title: The algorithm that votes
mcc_363.body: Your internal AI starts "voting" in internal surveys. It always answers "more coffee."

choices.A: Shut it down and audit immediately.
choices.B: Keep it: it is funny, right? No.
choices.C: Limit AI + create a strict usage framework.

mcc_364.title: Citizen label of the month
mcc_364.body: A ministry offers you a citizen label of the month if you make a video about taxation.

choices.A: Make a simple educational video, no cringe.
choices.C: Make a clear article + infographics, not TikTok.

mcc_365.title: Meeting... about meetings
mcc_365.body: A meeting is organized to reduce meetings. It lasts 2 hours. Peak irony.

choices.A: Stop it immediately and set anti-meeting rules.
choices.B: Let it run: "it will end somehow."
choices.C: Do a 15-minute summary and cancel the rest.

mcc_366.title: Diplomatic sandwich
mcc_366.body: During an official visit, you shake hands with a minister... while holding a sandwich.

choices.A: Own it with humor + serious message behind it.
choices.B: Try to get the photo removed, Streisand effect.
choices.C: Publish a concrete action to change the subject.

mcc_367.title: Anti-jargon law
mcc_367.body: In a meeting, everyone is speaking in corporate jargon.

choices.A: Rewrite everything in simple language, good.
choices.B: Ignore it: that is the culture.
choices.C: Keep the jargon... but add a ridiculous glossary.

mcc_368.title: Pigeon lobby
mcc_368.body: Pigeons settle on your headquarters and become a "symbol of urban resistance."

choices.A: Install gentle solutions + cleaning + communication.
choices.B: Chase them away harshly, risking backlash.
choices.C: Create a biodiversity roof and move the problem.

mcc_369.title: Live mic test
mcc_369.body: During a public meeting, the mic stays on. You hear your CFO whisper: "we're doomed."

choices.A: Transparency + joke + clarify the context.
choices.B: Cut the live feed and deny it, disaster.
choices.C: Own it + announce a reassuring measure.

mcc_370.title: Regulatory dress code
mcc_370.body: A regulator proposes a "moderation" dress code: gray suit required.

choices.A: Follow it only for official meetings.
choices.C: Wear gray, but keep one discreet detail to show your disagreement.

mcc_371.title: Candidate in cosplay
mcc_371.body: A candidate arrives at your factory dressed as a "worker of the future."

choices.A: Stay neutral + focus on facts.
choices.C: Offer a serious technical visit to bring things back on track.

mcc_372.title: Petition for fewer logos
mcc_372.body: An internal petition asks to reduce logos everywhere.

choices.A: Tone it down: more discreet branding.
choices.C: Let teams vote on which items to de-logo.

mcc_373.title: Law against dark patterns
mcc_373.body: A law targets deceptive interfaces. Your designers literally named one button "final trap."

choices.A: Redesign the interface to make it simple and honest.
choices.C: Redesign only the most visible problem areas.

mcc_374.title: Campaign office in your parking lot
mcc_374.body: A political party accidentally sets up a campaign office in your parking lot.

choices.A: Politely ask them to leave + stay neutral.
choices.B: Let them stay: more people, right?
choices.C: Offer an alternative municipal room and help them move.

mcc_375.title: Debate on memes
mcc_375.body: A lawmaker wants to regulate memes. Your company is often becoming the target of online jokes.

choices.A: Respond with humor + stay professional.
choices.C: Launch an official meme, careful.

mcc_376.title: No-AC day in peak heat
mcc_376.body: You are asked to turn off the air conditioning to set an example. It is 38°C and the employees are taking it very badly.

choices.A: Smart moderation: thresholds, ventilation, adapted hours.
choices.C: Take part symbolically for 1 hour and offer solutions, water, breaks.

mcc_377.title: Poorly configured drone
mcc_377.body: Your security drone films the sky for three days instead of monitoring the area.

choices.A: Audit and fix it + transparency.
choices.C: Suspend drones while you review the policy.

mcc_378.title: Debate on the right to make mistakes
mcc_378.body: A law encourages the right to make mistakes.

choices.A: Set up a fast correction process + transparency.
choices.C: Train teams + double validation.

mcc_379.title: Anti-noise committee
mcc_379.body: An anti-noise committee criticizes your open spaces. They measured: "too many angry keyboards."

choices.A: Create quiet areas + rules.
choices.C: Broader remote work.

mcc_380.title: Compliment day
mcc_380.body: A national initiative requires giving 3 compliments at work.

choices.A: Play along: positive culture, cool.
choices.C: Do a voluntary version, not mandatory.

mcc_381.title: Chatbot answering instead of the team
mcc_381.body: Your internal chatbot starts replying to journalists instead of your team.

choices.A: Shut it down immediately + apologize + audit.
choices.C: Limit access and add strict safeguards.

mcc_382.title: Baguette plan
mcc_382.body: To calm a crisis, an elected official proposes subsidizing baguettes.

choices.A: Support it through targeted donations, not politics.
choices.C: Support it through an internal, local food assistance program.

mcc_383.title: Suit launching confetti
mcc_383.body: Your test "connected" suit starts launching confetti during a parliamentary hearing.

choices.A: Laugh + apologize + promise a technical investigation.
choices.C: Cut all public tech and stay classic.

mcc_384.title: Debate on a success tax
mcc_384.body: A debate proposes a "tax on success." Your board proposes a "tax on failure" instead. No.

choices.A: Join the debate with data and proposals.
choices.C: Stay neutral and reinforce your concrete social actions.

mcc_385.title: Misunderstood slogan
mcc_385.body: Your slogan "Always simpler" is taken as "always more."

choices.A: Clarify with humor + restrained message.
choices.C: Ignore it: it will pass.

mcc_386.title: Minister wants an escape room
mcc_386.body: A minister wants to discover your business through a gimmicky setup.

choices.A: Create a professional educational escape room.
choices.C: Do a classic demo, but fun and short.

mcc_387.title: Regulators ask for a jingle
mcc_387.body: A regulator asks you for a compliance jingle "to raise awareness."

choices.A: Make a simple, useful jingle, not cringe.
choices.C: Do an internal campaign without a jingle, just training.

mcc_388.title: Debate on wait lines
mcc_388.body: Your support line has a 40-minute wait. An elected official says: "this is a democratic issue." Okay.

choices.A: Strengthen support + public response-time goals.
choices.B: Put in a bot that just says "please wait."
choices.C: Create a paid priority support line, risky.

mcc_389.title: Anti-corporate-bullshit law
mcc_389.body: A law wants to ban corporate bullshit.

choices.A: Go back to simple, measurable goals.
choices.C: Keep the vision... but add concrete numbers.

mcc_390.title: Debate on office plants
mcc_390.body: A political initiative requires one plant per employee.

choices.A: Plants yes, but appropriate + allergy-safe options.
choices.C: Virtual plants, desktop wallpaper.

mcc_391.title: Parliamentary ping-pong match
mcc_391.body: You are offered a charity ping-pong match against a lawmaker.

choices.A: Accept: good image + charity.
choices.C: Accept, but fund a neutral project

mcc_392.title: Internal vote on national pizza
mcc_392.body: A political debate erupts over the "national" pizza.

choices.A: Organize a tasting and calm everyone down.
choices.C: Let it happen: "it brings people together," right.

mcc_393.title: Scooter checks
mcc_393.body: The city is cracking down on scooters.

choices.A: Create a structured soft-mobility plan.
choices.C: Ban scooters on site, cue the anger.

mcc_394.title: Anti-neon-green-button law
mcc_394.body: A law wants to ban neon green buttons that are "too pushy."

choices.A: Redesign with accessibility and calm colors.
choices.B: Change just one button and hope.
choices.C: Switch to neon blue, same problem.

mcc_395.title: Official honk announcement
mcc_395.body: A minister wants to announce your partnership with a ridiculous stunt.

choices.A: Make a sober announcement, no show.
choices.B: Find a horn and embrace the show.
choices.C: Put on a show... but a useful one: numbers + commitments.

mcc_396.title: Debate on forbidden words
mcc_396.body: One party proposes banning the word "profitability" on TV. Your CFO goes silent.

choices.A: Talk about economic sustainability and investment.
choices.B: Keep saying profitability and own it.
choices.C: Stop speaking in public, just publish reports.

mcc_397.title: Intern becomes spokesperson
mcc_397.body: Your communications intern accidentally replies to a minister online.

choices.A: Own it: apologies + supervision + training.
choices.C: Say it was intentional, a lie.

mcc_398.title: Debate on glitter
mcc_398.body: A law wants to ban glitter because of pollution. Your annual event is called "Glitter Summit." Oops.

choices.A: Rename it + zero glitter + eco plan.
choices.C: Keep the name but no glitter.

mcc_399.title: Great silence
mcc_399.body: To calm tensions, an elected official proposes a national minute of silence... on social media.

choices.A: Respect it: pause comms and post a sober message.
choices.C: Respect it, but prepare a useful action afterward.

mcc_400.title: Clown of capitalism
mcc_400.body: A commentator calls you a "clown of capitalism."

choices.A: Reply calmly with facts and actions.
choices.B: Reply with a red nose, dangerous but funny.
choices.C: Reply with humor + announce a concrete measure.

mcc_401.title: Minister wants a dark mode
mcc_401.body: A minister demands that all services have a "dark mode" to reduce civic fatigue.

choices.A: Create a real dark mode, sober + accessible.
choices.B: Keep the neon pink: "it's our signature."
choices.C: Dark mode, but subscribers only, ouch.

mcc_402.title: Strike... by the printers
mcc_402.body: The printers at headquarters start throwing errors on their own.

choices.A: Reduce printing + simpler processes.
choices.C: Create an "anti-useless-email charter," symbolic but useful.

mcc_403.title: Debate on the right to bug
mcc_403.body: A lawmaker proposes a "right to bugs": every citizen accepts 1 bug per month.

choices.A: Improve quality + transparency on incidents.
choices.B: Exploit the rule: "monthly bug, it's legal."
choices.C: Launch a public bug bounty program.

mcc_404.title: QR code on the forehead
mcc_404.body: The communications team wants to print a QR code on employees' foreheads. HR is screaming.

choices.A: Ban it immediately: respect and common sense.
choices.B: Allow it only in an optional and respectful form.
choices.C: Replace it with an optional digital badge.

mcc_405.title: Board meeting
mcc_405.body: A board member says meetings should be in ASMR to lower stress.

choices.A: Refuse: normal meetings.
choices.B: Test it on 1 meeting, for fun.
choices.C: Reduce meeting length: real anti-stress.

mcc_406.title: Debate on captchas
mcc_406.body: A lawmaker wants "cultural" captchas, like: "click on the baguette."

choices.A: Refuse: international accessibility.
choices.C: Propose neutral, inclusive captchas.

mcc_407.title: Mustache plan
mcc_407.body: To support a cause, you are offered a company-wide mustache plan. Your brand image is trembling.

choices.A: Support through donations and actions, not mustaches.
choices.B: Mustaches for all: guaranteed to go viral.
choices.C: Voluntary mustaches + sober message.

mcc_408.title: Silence of notifications
mcc_408.body: A law wants to impose one daily hour without notifications.

choices.A: Calm mode by default + user respect.
choices.C: Calm mode, but opt-out possible, balance.

mcc_409.title: Ergonomic chair lobby
mcc_409.body: A lobby demands "Republic-certified" ergonomic chairs. Your offices have chairs... from 2009.

choices.A: Invest in ergonomics, health + proof.
choices.C: Broader remote work to reduce the need.

mcc_410.title: TV spot... filmed in your fridge
mcc_410.body: An agency proposes shooting an ad inside your fridge "to show freshness."

choices.A: Refuse: keep a professional image.
choices.C: Accept, but shoot in a clean kitchen.

mcc_411.title: Anti-English debate
mcc_411.body: An elected official criticizes English in the workplace.

choices.A: Go back to simple English in French internally.
choices.C: Clean bilingual approach: terms explained and accessible.

mcc_412.title: Cafeteria referendum
mcc_412.body: Employees launch a referendum: kebab or salad. Investors ask: "and the margin?"

choices.A: Balanced option: choice + nutrition.
choices.B: Kebab every day, for the vibe.
choices.C: Salad only, revolt.

mcc_413.title: Lawmaker wants to test your service
mcc_413.body: A lawmaker wants to test your service live in Parliament. He cannot click. The screen is upside down.

choices.A: Help gently + ultra-simple guide.
choices.B: Let him figure it out, terrible signal.
choices.C: Offer a clear pre-recorded demo.

mcc_414.title: Listening company label
mcc_414.body: An association offers a "Listening Company" label if you pay. It feels like a trap.

choices.A: Refuse and invest in real internal measures.
choices.C: Negotiate: real audit, label only if results are real.

mcc_415.title: Debate on reminder emails
mcc_415.body: A proposal wants to limit reminder emails. Your tool sends 14 a day to each customer.

choices.A: Reduce them drastically + user preferences.
choices.C: Keep them, but make unsubscribing ultra simple.

mcc_416.title: Solidarity fund
mcc_416.body: A minister launches a fund for sad startups.

choices.A: Refuse: not ethical.
choices.C: Participate as a donor, not as a beneficiary.

mcc_417.title: Site outage... because of a cat
mcc_417.body: A cat sits on a keyboard in the datacenter and cuts a service. An elected official talks about "national fragility."

choices.A: Strengthen procedures + communicate honestly.
choices.C: Own the cat + invest in redundancy.

mcc_418.title: Weak emergency plan
mcc_418.body: In the middle of a crisis, your team is mainly proposing motivational stickers instead of a real plan.

choices.A: Concrete plan, no stickers
choices.B: Put stickers everywhere to boost morale
choices.C: Concrete plan with a small visual support

mcc_419.title: Debate on priority lines
mcc_419.body: A law bans paid priority lines. Your VIP service is... exactly that.

choices.A: Remove VIP and strengthen overall support.
choices.B: Rename VIP to "comfort" to work around the law.
choices.C: Keep VIP, but also create fast standard support.

mcc_420.title: Compliment week for the
mcc_420.body: You are advised to send an overly flattering message to the regulators. Your lawyer rejects the idea immediately.

choices.A: Stay neutral, just cooperate properly.
choices.C: Send a clear, useful report, the best compliment.

mcc_421.title: Debate against infinite growth
mcc_421.body: A TV show asks whether you believe in infinite growth.

choices.A: Balanced position: growth + moderation + proof.
choices.C: Limited growth and priority on stability.

mcc_422.title: The CEO's suit becomes a meme
mcc_422.body: Your suit has become a political meme.

choices.A: Ignore it and stay professional.
choices.C: Change style + refocus on actions.

mcc_423.title: Elevator reform
mcc_423.body: A law wants elevators to be more inclusive. Your HQ has one that speaks... in Latin.

choices.A: Modernize: accessibility + compliance.
choices.C: Modernize partially, voice + signage.

mcc_424.title: Debate on the right to a break
mcc_424.body: People want to impose a 10-minute break every hour. Support says "thank you," finance says "no."

choices.A: Apply it + reorganize schedules.
choices.C: Apply it only to the most stressful jobs.

mcc_425.title: Confetti budget
mcc_425.body: The ad team wants a confetti budget for visibility.

choices.A: Refuse and invest in product/service.
choices.C: Tiny confetti budget + big quality budget.

mcc_426.title: Debate on project names
mcc_426.body: A commission criticizes your project names: "Nebula," "Hydra," "Phoenix."

choices.A: Rename them with simple, clear names.
choices.C: Keep them, but add a readable description.

mcc_427.title: No-jargon day
mcc_427.body: A national no-jargon day is coming. Your team wonders how to speak more simply.

choices.A: Train people to speak simply: success.
choices.C: Allow a little jargon, but with simple explanations.

mcc_428.title: Lawmaker wants a button
mcc_428.body: Your product team says it is doable.

choices.A: Add an optional wellness button.
choices.C: Add the button... but it opens the payment page, no.

mcc_429.title: Debate on colors
mcc_429.body: You are accused of using the color of "one side."

choices.A: Stay neutral and change nothing.
choices.C: Reaffirm neutrality + refocus on the product.

mcc_430.title: Tax on long words
mcc_430.body: An elected official proposes a tax on words longer than 12 letters. Your legal team vanishes.

choices.A: Simplify communication everywhere.
choices.C: Create short versions + legal annexes.

mcc_431.title: Debate on sensitive data
mcc_431.body: A journalist asks whether you collect sensitive data. Your data team says: "depends what you mean by sensitive."

choices.A: Audit and reduce collected data.
choices.C: Transparency + clear consent before collection.

mcc_432.title: Happy bug day
mcc_432.body: Your team wants to celebrate bugs with cakes.

choices.A: Cakes + quality sprint, balance.
choices.C: Ban the cakes: immediate sadness.

mcc_433.title: Micro-listening plan
mcc_433.body: A minister wants to reduce city noise.

choices.A: Refuse: too sensitive.
choices.C: Accept, but only with anonymization and total transparency.

mcc_434.title: Debate on gifts to elected officials
mcc_434.body: Someone suggests sending gift baskets to elected officials.

choices.A: Refuse: strict no-gifts policy.
choices.C: Make a public donation to a charity instead.

mcc_435.title: World PowerPoint Summit
mcc_435.body: You are invited to the World PowerPoint Summit.

choices.A: Go for networking.
choices.C: Go, but send someone else, not you.

mcc_436.title: Anti-tab law
mcc_436.body: A proposal wants to limit the number of browser tabs open at work, for mental health.

choices.A: Train people and reduce cognitive load.
choices.C: Add organization tools, and let the tabs stay.

mcc_437.title: Debate on a 3-day weekend
mcc_437.body: The 3-day weekend is becoming a political topic.

choices.A: Test it in one pilot department.
choices.C: Compromise: flexible hours and rotating days off.

mcc_438.title: Regulator wants an emoji
mcc_438.body: A regulator asks for an official compliance emoji in apps. Your designer suggests 🧾.

choices.A: Cooperate: use a clear, neutral symbol.
choices.C: Create a serious internal label, not an emoji.

mcc_439.title: Debate on prices
mcc_439.body: You are accused of "manipulating" people with .99 pricing.

choices.A: Switch to simple, transparent pricing.
choices.C: Comparative test: round prices in some markets.

mcc_440.title: Committee on absurd practices
mcc_440.body: A parliamentary committee wants to study certain absurd business practices.

choices.A: Share 5 examples + solutions.
choices.C: Share everything: total transparency.

mcc_441.title: Debate on logo modesty
mcc_441.body: A proposal suggests hiding logos that are too visible in public.

choices.A: Discreet branding, quality first.
choices.B: Work around it with a giant "discreet" logo.
choices.C: Reduce logo presence on unnecessary items.

mcc_442.title: Crisis of boxes too strong
mcc_442.body: Your boxes are so sturdy they do not fold.

choices.A: Change the packaging + apologize + test.
choices.C: Add a simple "how to fold" guide.

mcc_443.title: Minister wants a tutorial
mcc_443.body: You are asked to make a national tutorial on "how to create a password." Your audience: ages 8 to 88.

choices.A: Make a simple, useful, inclusive tutorial.
choices.C: Make a tutorial packed with jargon, no.

mcc_444.title: Debate on productivity
mcc_444.body: An elected official talks about emotional productivity.

choices.A: No happiness KPI: real support for well-being.
choices.C: Light anonymous survey + concrete actions.

mcc_445.title: Anti-cognitive-overload law
mcc_445.body: Your dashboard looks like a cockpit: too much information, too many buttons.

choices.A: Simplify the interface: fewer elements, but better ones.
choices.C: Add a "simple" mode for everyone.

choices.A: Short standing meetings: okay.
choices.C: Mixed: standing for quick topics, seated for heavy ones.

mcc_447.title: Debate on silent drones
mcc_447.body: People want to ban noisy drones.

choices.A: Fix it: quiet, compliant drone.
choices.B: Keep the meowing: it is adorable.
choices.C: Limit drones to industrial zones.

mcc_448.title: Smart pen scandal
mcc_448.body: Your smart pen is sending data.

choices.A: Transparency + clear consent + stop unnecessary collection.
choices.C: Pull the product from the market.

mcc_449.title: Debate on the right to complain
mcc_449.body: A political party proposes an official right to complain. Your support gets 10,000 complaints a day already. What changes?

choices.A: Improve support + create a clear feedback channel.
choices.B: Ignore it: it is just noise.
choices.C: Create a "complain" button that opens a short form.

mcc_450.title: Parliament wants your animation
mcc_450.body: Parliament asks you for an official animated visual for its campaign.

choices.A: Refuse: stay sober.
choices.B: Accept: dancing llama, huge media buzz.
choices.C: Offer a simple, professional visual, no llama.
